entries.name: setMeter
entries.description:
Sets the pixels to meter scale factor.

All coordinates in the physics module are divided by this number and converted to meters, and it creates a convenient way to draw the objects directly to the screen without the need for graphics transformations.

It is recommended to create shapes no larger than 10 times the scale. This is important because Box2D is tuned to work well with shape sizes from 0.1 to 10 meters. The default meter scale is 30.

arguments.name: scale
arguments.description: The scale factor as an integer.

entries.name: quit
entries.description: Callback function triggered when the game is closed.

returns.name: r
returns.description: Abort quitting. If true, do not close the game.

entries.name: resize
entries.description: Called when the window is resized, for example if the user resizes the window, or if love.window.setMode is called with an unsupported width or height in fullscreen and the window chooses the closest appropriate size.

variants.description:
Calls to love.window.setMode will '''only''' trigger this event if the width or height of the window after the call doesn't match the requested width and height. This can happen if a fullscreen mode is requested which doesn't match any supported mode, or if the fullscreen type is 'desktop' and the requested width or height don't match the desktop resolution.

Since 11.0, this function returns width and height in DPI-scaled units rather than pixels.

arguments.name: w
arguments.description: The new width.

arguments.name: h
arguments.description: The new height.

entries.name: run
entries.description: The main function, containing the main loop. A sensible default is used when left out.

returns.name: mainLoop
returns.description: Function which handlers one frame, including events and rendering when called.

entries.name: setDeprecationOutput
entries.description:
Sets whether LÖVE displays warnings when using deprecated functionality. It is disabled by default in fused mode, and enabled by default otherwise.

When deprecation output is enabled, the first use of a formally deprecated LÖVE API will show a message at the bottom of the screen for a short time, and print the message to the console.

arguments.name: enable
arguments.description: Whether to enable or disable deprecation output.

entries.name: newDecoder
entries.description: Attempts to find a decoder for the encoded sound data in the specified file.

arguments.name: file
arguments.description: The file with encoded sound data.

arguments.name: buffer
arguments.description: The size of each decoded chunk, in bytes.

returns.name: decoder
returns.description: A new Decoder object.

arguments.name: filename
arguments.description: The filename of the file with encoded sound data.

arguments.name: buffer
arguments.description: The size of each decoded chunk, in bytes.

returns.name: decoder
returns.description: A new Decoder object.

entries.name: newSoundData
entries.description:
Creates new SoundData from a filepath, File, or Decoder. It's also possible to create SoundData with a custom sample rate, channel and bit depth.

The sound data will be decoded to the memory in a raw format. It is recommended to create only short sounds like effects, as a 3 minute song uses 30 MB of memory this way.

arguments.name: filename
arguments.description: The file name of the file to load.

returns.name: soundData
returns.description: A new SoundData object.

arguments.name: file
arguments.description: A File pointing to an audio file.

returns.name: soundData
returns.description: A new SoundData object.

arguments.name: decoder
arguments.description: Decode data from this Decoder until EOF.

returns.name: soundData
returns.description: A new SoundData object.

arguments.name: samples
arguments.description: Total number of samples.

arguments.name: rate
arguments.description: Number of samples per second

arguments.name: bits
arguments.description: Bits per sample (8 or 16).

arguments.name: channels
arguments.description: Either 1 for mono or 2 for stereo.

returns.name: soundData
returns.description: A new SoundData object.

entries.name: getClipboardText
entries.description: Gets text from the clipboard.

returns.name: text
returns.description: The text currently held in the system's clipboard.

entries.name: getOS
entries.description: Gets the current operating system. In general, LÖVE abstracts away the need to know the current operating system, but there are a few cases where it can be useful (especially in combination with os.execute.)

variants.description: In LÖVE version 0.8.0, the '''love._os''' string contains the current operating system.

returns.name: osString
returns.description: The current operating system. 'OS X', 'Windows', 'Linux', 'Android' or 'iOS'.

entries.name: getPowerInfo
entries.description: Gets information about the system's power supply.

returns.name: state
returns.description: The basic state of the power supply.

returns.name: percent
returns.description: Percentage of battery life left, between 0 and 100. nil if the value can't be determined or there's no battery.

returns.name: seconds
returns.description: Seconds of battery life left. nil if the value can't be determined or there's no battery.

entries.name: getProcessorCount
entries.description: Gets the amount of logical processor in the system.

variants.description: The number includes the threads reported if technologies such as Intel's Hyper-threading are enabled. For example, on a 4-core CPU with Hyper-threading, this function will return 8.

returns.name: processorCount
returns.description: Amount of logical processors.

entries.name: hasBackgroundMusic
entries.description:
Gets whether another application on the system is playing music in the background.

Currently this is implemented on iOS and Android, and will always return false on other operating systems. The t.audio.mixwithsystem flag in love.conf can be used to configure whether background audio / music from other apps should play while LÖVE is open.

returns.name: backgroundmusic
returns.description: True if the user is playing music in the background via another app, false otherwise.

entries.name: openURL
entries.description: Opens a URL with the user's web or file browser.

variants.description: Passing file:// scheme in Android 7.0 (Nougat) and later always result in failure. Prior to 11.2, this will crash LÖVE instead of returning false.

arguments.name: url
arguments.description: The URL to open. Must be formatted as a proper URL.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the URL was opened successfully.

entries.name: setClipboardText
entries.description: Puts text in the clipboard.

arguments.name: text
arguments.description: The new text to hold in the system's clipboard.

entries.name: vibrate
entries.description: Causes the device to vibrate, if possible. Currently this will only work on Android and iOS devices that have a built-in vibration motor.

arguments.name: seconds
arguments.description: The duration to vibrate for. If called on an iOS device, it will always vibrate for 0.5 seconds due to limitations in the iOS system APIs.

entries.name: textedited
entries.description:
Called when the candidate text for an IME (Input Method Editor) has changed.

The candidate text is not the final text that the user will eventually choose. Use love.textinput for that.

arguments.name: text
arguments.description: The UTF-8 encoded unicode candidate text.

arguments.name: start
arguments.description: The start cursor of the selected candidate text.

arguments.name: length
arguments.description: The length of the selected candidate text. May be 0.

entries.name: textinput
entries.description: Called when text has been entered by the user. For example if shift-2 is pressed on an American keyboard layout, the text '@' will be generated.

variants.description:
Although Lua strings can store UTF-8 encoded unicode text just fine, many functions in Lua's string library will not treat the text as you might expect. For example, #text (and string.len(text)) will give the number of ''bytes'' in the string, rather than the number of unicode characters. The Lua wiki and a presentation by one of Lua's creators give more in-depth explanations, with some tips.

The utf8 library can be used to operate on UTF-8 encoded unicode text (such as the text argument given in this function.)

On Android and iOS, textinput is disabled by default; call love.keyboard.setTextInput to enable it.

arguments.name: text
arguments.description: The UTF-8 encoded unicode text.

entries.name: getChannel
entries.description: Creates or retrieves a named thread channel.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the channel you want to create or retrieve.

returns.name: channel
returns.description: The Channel object associated with the name.

entries.name: newChannel
entries.description:
Create a new unnamed thread channel.

One use for them is to pass new unnamed channels to other threads via Channel:push on a named channel.

returns.name: channel
returns.description: The new Channel object.

entries.name: newThread
entries.description: Creates a new Thread from a filename, string or FileData object containing Lua code.

arguments.name: filename
arguments.description: The name of the Lua file to use as the source.

returns.name: thread
returns.description: A new Thread that has yet to be started.

arguments.name: fileData
arguments.description: The FileData containing the Lua code to use as the source.

returns.name: thread
returns.description: A new Thread that has yet to be started.

arguments.name: codestring
arguments.description: A string containing the Lua code to use as the source. It needs to either be at least 1024 characters long, or contain at least one newline.

returns.name: thread
returns.description: A new Thread that has yet to be started.

entries.name: threaderror
entries.description: Callback function triggered when a Thread encounters an error.

arguments.name: thread
arguments.description: The thread which produced the error.

arguments.name: errorstr
arguments.description: The error message.

entries.name: getAverageDelta
entries.description: Returns the average delta time (seconds per frame) over the last second.

returns.name: delta
returns.description: The average delta time over the last second.

entries.name: getDelta
entries.description: Returns the time between the last two frames.

returns.name: dt
returns.description: The time passed (in seconds).

entries.name: getFPS
entries.description: Returns the current frames per second.

returns.name: fps
returns.description: The current FPS.

entries.name: getTime
entries.description:
Returns the value of a timer with an unspecified starting time.

This function should only be used to calculate differences between points in time, as the starting time of the timer is unknown.

returns.name: time
returns.description: The time in seconds. Given as a decimal, accurate to the microsecond.

entries.name: sleep
entries.description: Pauses the current thread for the specified amount of time.

arguments.name: s
arguments.description: Seconds to sleep for.

entries.name: step
entries.description:
Measures the time between two frames.

Calling this changes the return value of love.timer.getDelta.

returns.name: dt
returns.description: The time passed (in seconds).

entries.name: getPosition
entries.description: Gets the current position of the specified touch-press, in pixels.

variants.description: The unofficial Android and iOS ports of LÖVE 0.9.2 reported touch-press positions as normalized values in the range of 1, whereas this API reports positions in pixels.

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: The identifier of the touch-press. Use love.touch.getTouches, love.touchpressed, or love.touchmoved to obtain touch id values.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The position along the x-axis of the touch-press inside the window, in pixels.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The position along the y-axis of the touch-press inside the window, in pixels.

entries.name: getPressure
entries.description: Gets the current pressure of the specified touch-press.

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: The identifier of the touch-press. Use love.touch.getTouches, love.touchpressed, or love.touchmoved to obtain touch id values.

returns.name: pressure
returns.description: The pressure of the touch-press. Most touch screens aren't pressure sensitive, in which case the pressure will be 1.

entries.name: getTouches
entries.description: Gets a list of all active touch-presses.

variants.description:
The id values are the same as those used as arguments to love.touchpressed, love.touchmoved, and love.touchreleased.

The id value of a specific touch-press is only guaranteed to be unique for the duration of that touch-press. As soon as love.touchreleased is called using that id, it may be reused for a new touch-press via love.touchpressed.

returns.name: touches
returns.description: A list of active touch-press id values, which can be used with love.touch.getPosition.

entries.name: touchmoved
entries.description: Callback function triggered when a touch press moves inside the touch screen.

variants.description:
The identifier is only guaranteed to be unique for the specific touch press until love.touchreleased is called with that identifier, at which point it may be reused for new touch presses.

The unofficial Android and iOS ports of LÖVE 0.9.2 reported touch positions as normalized values in the range of 1, whereas this API reports positions in pixels.

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: The identifier for the touch press.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x-axis position of the touch inside the window, in pixels.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y-axis position of the touch inside the window, in pixels.

arguments.name: dx
arguments.description: The x-axis movement of the touch inside the window, in pixels.

arguments.name: dy
arguments.description: The y-axis movement of the touch inside the window, in pixels.

arguments.name: pressure
arguments.description: The amount of pressure being applied. Most touch screens aren't pressure sensitive, in which case the pressure will be 1.

entries.name: touchpressed
entries.description: Callback function triggered when the touch screen is touched.

variants.description:
The identifier is only guaranteed to be unique for the specific touch press until love.touchreleased is called with that identifier, at which point it may be reused for new touch presses.

The unofficial Android and iOS ports of LÖVE 0.9.2 reported touch positions as normalized values in the range of 1, whereas this API reports positions in pixels.

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: The identifier for the touch press.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x-axis position of the touch press inside the window, in pixels.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y-axis position of the touch press inside the window, in pixels.

arguments.name: dx
arguments.description: The x-axis movement of the touch press inside the window, in pixels. This should always be zero.

arguments.name: dy
arguments.description: The y-axis movement of the touch press inside the window, in pixels. This should always be zero.

arguments.name: pressure
arguments.description: The amount of pressure being applied. Most touch screens aren't pressure sensitive, in which case the pressure will be 1.

entries.name: touchreleased
entries.description: Callback function triggered when the touch screen stops being touched.

variants.description:
The identifier is only guaranteed to be unique for the specific touch press until love.touchreleased is called with that identifier, at which point it may be reused for new touch presses.

The unofficial Android and iOS ports of LÖVE 0.9.2 reported touch positions as normalized values in the range of 1, whereas this API reports positions in pixels.

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: The identifier for the touch press.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x-axis position of the touch inside the window, in pixels.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y-axis position of the touch inside the window, in pixels.

arguments.name: dx
arguments.description: The x-axis movement of the touch inside the window, in pixels.

arguments.name: dy
arguments.description: The y-axis movement of the touch inside the window, in pixels.

arguments.name: pressure
arguments.description: The amount of pressure being applied. Most touch screens aren't pressure sensitive, in which case the pressure will be 1.

entries.name: update
entries.description: Callback function used to update the state of the game every frame.

arguments.name: dt
arguments.description: Time since the last update in seconds.

entries.name: newVideoStream
entries.description: Creates a new VideoStream. Currently only Ogg Theora video files are supported. VideoStreams can't draw videos, see love.graphics.newVideo for that.

arguments.name: filename
arguments.description: The file path to the Ogg Theora video file.

returns.name: videostream
returns.description: A new VideoStream.

arguments.name: file
arguments.description: The File object containing the Ogg Theora video.

returns.name: videostream
returns.description: A new VideoStream.

entries.name: visible
entries.description: Callback function triggered when window is minimized/hidden or unminimized by the user.

arguments.name: visible
arguments.description: True if the window is visible, false if it isn't.

entries.name: wheelmoved
entries.description: Callback function triggered when the mouse wheel is moved.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: Amount of horizontal mouse wheel movement. Positive values indicate movement to the right.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Amount of vertical mouse wheel movement. Positive values indicate upward movement.

entries.name: close
entries.description: Closes the window. It can be reopened with love.window.setMode.

entries.name: fromPixels
entries.description:
Converts a number from pixels to density-independent units.

The pixel density inside the window might be greater (or smaller) than the 'size' of the window. For example on a retina screen in Mac OS X with the highdpi window flag enabled, the window may take up the same physical size as an 800x600 window, but the area inside the window uses 1600x1200 pixels. love.window.fromPixels(1600) would return 800 in that case.

This function converts coordinates from pixels to the size users are expecting them to display at onscreen. love.window.toPixels does the opposite. The highdpi window flag must be enabled to use the full pixel density of a Retina screen on Mac OS X and iOS. The flag currently does nothing on Windows and Linux, and on Android it is effectively always enabled.

Most LÖVE functions return values and expect arguments in terms of pixels rather than density-independent units.

arguments.name: pixelvalue
arguments.description: A number in pixels to convert to density-independent units.

returns.name: value
returns.description: The converted number, in density-independent units.

variants.description: The units of love.graphics.getWidth, love.graphics.getHeight, love.mouse.getPosition, mouse events, love.touch.getPosition, and touch events are always in terms of pixels.

arguments.name: px
arguments.description: The x-axis value of a coordinate in pixels.

arguments.name: py
arguments.description: The y-axis value of a coordinate in pixels.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The converted x-axis value of the coordinate, in density-independent units.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The converted y-axis value of the coordinate, in density-independent units.

entries.name: getDesktopDimensions
entries.description: Gets the width and height of the desktop.

arguments.name: displayindex
arguments.description: The index of the display, if multiple monitors are available.

returns.name: width
returns.description: The width of the desktop.

returns.name: height
returns.description: The height of the desktop.

entries.name: getDisplayCount
entries.description: Gets the number of connected monitors.

returns.name: count
returns.description: The number of currently connected displays.

entries.name: getDisplayName
entries.description: Gets the name of a display.

arguments.name: displayindex
arguments.description: The index of the display to get the name of.

returns.name: name
returns.description: The name of the specified display.

entries.name: getDisplayOrientation
entries.description: Gets current device display orientation.

arguments.name: displayindex
arguments.description: Display index to get its display orientation, or nil for default display index.

returns.name: orientation
returns.description: Current device display orientation.

entries.name: getDPIScale
entries.description:
Gets the DPI scale factor associated with the window.

The pixel density inside the window might be greater (or smaller) than the 'size' of the window. For example on a retina screen in Mac OS X with the highdpi window flag enabled, the window may take up the same physical size as an 800x600 window, but the area inside the window uses 1600x1200 pixels. love.window.getDPIScale() would return 2.0 in that case.

The love.window.fromPixels and love.window.toPixels functions can also be used to convert between units.

The highdpi window flag must be enabled to use the full pixel density of a Retina screen on Mac OS X and iOS. The flag currently does nothing on Windows and Linux, and on Android it is effectively always enabled.

variants.description: The units of love.graphics.getWidth, love.graphics.getHeight, love.mouse.getPosition, mouse events, love.touch.getPosition, and touch events are always in terms of pixels.

returns.name: scale
returns.description: The pixel scale factor associated with the window.

entries.name: getFullscreen
entries.description: Gets whether the window is fullscreen.

returns.name: fullscreen
returns.description: True if the window is fullscreen, false otherwise.

returns.name: fstype
returns.description: The type of fullscreen mode used.

entries.name: getFullscreenModes
entries.description: Gets a list of supported fullscreen modes.

arguments.name: displayindex
arguments.description: The index of the display, if multiple monitors are available.

returns.name: modes
returns.description: A table of width/height pairs. (Note that this may not be in order.)

table.name: width
table.description: Window fullscreen width.

table.name: height
table.description: Window fullscreen height.

entries.name: getIcon
entries.description: Gets the window icon.

returns.name: imagedata
returns.description: The window icon imagedata, or nil if no icon has been set with love.window.setIcon.

entries.name: getMode
entries.description: Gets the display mode and properties of the window.

returns.name: width
returns.description: Window width.

returns.name: height
returns.description: Window height.

returns.name: flags
returns.description: Table with the window properties:

table.name: fullscreen
table.description: Fullscreen (true), or windowed (false).

table.name: fullscreentype
table.description: The type of fullscreen mode used.

table.name: vsync
table.description: True if the graphics framerate is synchronized with the monitor's refresh rate, false otherwise.

table.name: msaa
table.description: The number of antialiasing samples used (0 if MSAA is disabled).

table.name: resizable
table.description: True if the window is resizable in windowed mode, false otherwise.

table.name: borderless
table.description: True if the window is borderless in windowed mode, false otherwise.

table.name: centered
table.description: True if the window is centered in windowed mode, false otherwise.

table.name: display
table.description: The index of the display the window is currently in, if multiple monitors are available.

table.name: minwidth
table.description: The minimum width of the window, if it's resizable.

table.name: minheight
table.description: The minimum height of the window, if it's resizable.

table.name: highdpi
table.description: True if high-dpi mode is allowed on Retina displays in OS X. Does nothing on non-Retina displays.

table.name: refreshrate
table.description: The refresh rate of the screen's current display mode, in Hz. May be 0 if the value can't be determined.

table.name: x
table.description: The x-coordinate of the window's position in its current display.

table.name: y
table.description: The y-coordinate of the window's position in its current display.

table.name: srgb
table.description: Removed in 0.10.0 (use love.graphics.isGammaCorrect instead). True if sRGB gamma correction is applied when drawing to the screen.

entries.name: getPosition
entries.description:
Gets the position of the window on the screen.

The window position is in the coordinate space of the display it is currently in.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The x-coordinate of the window's position.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The y-coordinate of the window's position.

returns.name: displayindex
returns.description: The index of the display that the window is in.

entries.name: getSafeArea
entries.description: Gets area inside the window which is known to be unobstructed by a system title bar, the iPhone X notch, etc. Useful for making sure UI elements can be seen by the user.

variants.description: Values returned are in DPI-scaled units (the same coordinate system as most other window-related APIs), not in pixels.

returns.name: x
returns.description: Starting position of safe area (x-axis).

returns.name: y
returns.description: Starting position of safe area (y-axis).

returns.name: w
returns.description: Width of safe area.

returns.name: h
returns.description: Height of safe area.

entries.name: getTitle
entries.description: Gets the window title.

returns.name: title
returns.description: The current window title.

entries.name: getVSync
entries.description: Gets current vertical synchronization (vsync).

variants.description: This can be less expensive alternative to love.window.getMode if you want to get current vsync status.

returns.name: vsync
returns.description: Current vsync status. 1 if enabled, 0 if disabled, and -1 for adaptive vsync.

entries.name: hasFocus
entries.description: Checks if the game window has keyboard focus.

returns.name: focus
returns.description: True if the window has the focus or false if not.

entries.name: hasMouseFocus
entries.description: Checks if the game window has mouse focus.

returns.name: focus
returns.description: True if the window has mouse focus or false if not.

entries.name: isDisplaySleepEnabled
entries.description:
Gets whether the display is allowed to sleep while the program is running.

Display sleep is disabled by default. Some types of input (e.g. joystick button presses) might not prevent the display from sleeping, if display sleep is allowed.

returns.name: enabled
returns.description: True if system display sleep is enabled / allowed, false otherwise.

entries.name: isMaximized
entries.description:
Gets whether the Window is currently maximized.

The window can be maximized if it is not fullscreen and is resizable, and either the user has pressed the window's Maximize button or love.window.maximize has been called.

returns.name: maximized
returns.description: True if the window is currently maximized in windowed mode, false otherwise.

entries.name: isMinimized
entries.description: Gets whether the Window is currently minimized.

returns.name: minimized
returns.description: True if the window is currently minimized, false otherwise.

entries.name: isOpen
entries.description: Checks if the window is open.

returns.name: open
returns.description: True if the window is open, false otherwise.

entries.name: isVisible
entries.description:
Checks if the game window is visible.

The window is considered visible if it's not minimized and the program isn't hidden.

returns.name: visible
returns.description: True if the window is visible or false if not.

entries.name: maximize
entries.description:
Makes the window as large as possible.

This function has no effect if the window isn't resizable, since it essentially programmatically presses the window's 'maximize' button.

entries.name: minimize
entries.description: Minimizes the window to the system's task bar / dock.

entries.name: requestAttention
entries.description:
Causes the window to request the attention of the user if it is not in the foreground.

In Windows the taskbar icon will flash, and in OS X the dock icon will bounce.

arguments.name: continuous
arguments.description: Whether to continuously request attention until the window becomes active, or to do it only once.

entries.name: restore
entries.description: Restores the size and position of the window if it was minimized or maximized.

entries.name: setDisplaySleepEnabled
entries.description:
Sets whether the display is allowed to sleep while the program is running.

Display sleep is disabled by default. Some types of input (e.g. joystick button presses) might not prevent the display from sleeping, if display sleep is allowed.

arguments.name: enable
arguments.description: True to enable system display sleep, false to disable it.

entries.name: setFullscreen
entries.description: Enters or exits fullscreen. The display to use when entering fullscreen is chosen based on which display the window is currently in, if multiple monitors are connected.

arguments.name: fullscreen
arguments.description: Whether to enter or exit fullscreen mode.

returns.name: success
returns.description: True if an attempt to enter fullscreen was successful, false otherwise.

variants.description: If fullscreen mode is entered and the window size doesn't match one of the monitor's display modes (in normal fullscreen mode) or the window size doesn't match the desktop size (in 'desktop' fullscreen mode), the window will be resized appropriately. The window will revert back to its original size again when fullscreen mode is exited using this function.

arguments.name: fullscreen
arguments.description: Whether to enter or exit fullscreen mode.

arguments.name: fstype
arguments.description: The type of fullscreen mode to use.

returns.name: success
returns.description: True if an attempt to enter fullscreen was successful, false otherwise.

entries.name: setIcon
entries.description: Sets the window icon until the game is quit. Not all operating systems support very large icon images.

arguments.name: imagedata
arguments.description: The window icon image.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the icon has been set successfully.

entries.name: setMode
entries.description:
Sets the display mode and properties of the window.

If width or height is 0, setMode will use the width and height of the desktop. 

Changing the display mode may have side effects: for example, canvases will be cleared and values sent to shaders with canvases beforehand or re-draw to them afterward if you need to.

variants.description:
* If fullscreen is enabled and the width or height is not supported (see resize event will be triggered.

* If the fullscreen type is 'desktop', then the window will be automatically resized to the desktop resolution.

* If the width and height is bigger than or equal to the desktop dimensions (this includes setting both to 0) and fullscreen is set to false, it will appear 'visually' fullscreen, but it's not true fullscreen and conf.lua (i.e. t.window = false) and use this function to manually create the window, then you must not call any other love.graphics.* function before this one. Doing so will result in undefined behavior and/or crashes because OpenGL cannot function properly without a window.

* Transparent backgrounds are currently not supported.

arguments.name: width
arguments.description: Display width.

arguments.name: height
arguments.description: Display height.

arguments.name: flags
arguments.description: The flags table with the options:

table.name: fullscreen
table.description: Fullscreen (true), or windowed (false).

table.name: fullscreentype
table.description: The type of fullscreen to use. This defaults to 'normal' in 0.9.0 through 0.9.2 and to 'desktop' in 0.10.0 and older.

table.name: vsync
table.description: True if LÖVE should wait for vsync, false otherwise.

table.name: msaa
table.description: The number of antialiasing samples.

table.name: stencil
table.description: Whether a stencil buffer should be allocated. If true, the stencil buffer will have 8 bits.

table.name: depth
table.description: The number of bits in the depth buffer.

table.name: resizable
table.description: True if the window should be resizable in windowed mode, false otherwise.

table.name: borderless
table.description: True if the window should be borderless in windowed mode, false otherwise.

table.name: centered
table.description: True if the window should be centered in windowed mode, false otherwise.

table.name: display
table.description: The index of the display to show the window in, if multiple monitors are available.

table.name: minwidth
table.description: The minimum width of the window, if it's resizable. Cannot be less than 1.

table.name: minheight
table.description: The minimum height of the window, if it's resizable. Cannot be less than 1.

table.name: highdpi
table.description: True if high-dpi mode should be used on Retina displays in macOS and iOS. Does nothing on non-Retina displays.

table.name: x
table.description: The x-coordinate of the window's position in the specified display.

table.name: y
table.description: The y-coordinate of the window's position in the specified display.

table.name: usedpiscale
table.description: Disables automatic DPI scaling when false.

table.name: srgb
table.description: Removed in 0.10.0 (set t.gammacorrect in conf.lua instead). True if sRGB gamma correction should be applied when drawing to the screen.

returns.name: success
returns.description: True if successful, false otherwise.

entries.name: setPosition
entries.description:
Sets the position of the window on the screen.

The window position is in the coordinate space of the specified display.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x-coordinate of the window's position.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y-coordinate of the window's position.

arguments.name: displayindex
arguments.description: The index of the display that the new window position is relative to.

entries.name: setTitle
entries.description: Sets the window title.

arguments.name: title
arguments.description: The new window title.

entries.name: setVSync
entries.description: Sets vertical synchronization mode.

variants.description:
* Not all graphics drivers support adaptive vsync (-1 value). In that case, it will be automatically set to 1.

* It is recommended to keep vsync activated if you don't know about the possible implications of turning it off.

* This function doesn't recreate the window, unlike love.window.setMode and love.window.updateMode.

arguments.name: vsync
arguments.description: VSync number: 1 to enable, 0 to disable, and -1 for adaptive vsync.

entries.name: showMessageBox
entries.description: Displays a message box dialog above the love window. The message box contains a title, optional text, and buttons.

variants.description: Displays a simple message box with a single 'OK' button.

arguments.name: title
arguments.description: The title of the message box.

arguments.name: message
arguments.description: The text inside the message box.

arguments.name: type
arguments.description: The type of the message box.

arguments.name: attachtowindow
arguments.description: Whether the message box should be attached to the love window or free-floating.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the message box was successfully displayed.

variants.description: Displays a message box with a customized list of buttons.

arguments.name: title
arguments.description: The title of the message box.

arguments.name: message
arguments.description: The text inside the message box.

arguments.name: buttonlist
arguments.description: A table containing a list of button names to show. The table can also contain the fields enterbutton and escapebutton, which should be the index of the default button to use when the user presses 'enter' or 'escape', respectively.

arguments.name: type
arguments.description: The type of the message box.

arguments.name: attachtowindow
arguments.description: Whether the message box should be attached to the love window or free-floating.

returns.name: pressedbutton
returns.description: The index of the button pressed by the user. May be 0 if the message box dialog was closed without pressing a button.

entries.name: toPixels
entries.description:
Converts a number from density-independent units to pixels.

The pixel density inside the window might be greater (or smaller) than the 'size' of the window. For example on a retina screen in Mac OS X with the highdpi window flag enabled, the window may take up the same physical size as an 800x600 window, but the area inside the window uses 1600x1200 pixels. love.window.toPixels(800) would return 1600 in that case.

This is used to convert coordinates from the size users are expecting them to display at onscreen to pixels. love.window.fromPixels does the opposite. The highdpi window flag must be enabled to use the full pixel density of a Retina screen on Mac OS X and iOS. The flag currently does nothing on Windows and Linux, and on Android it is effectively always enabled.

Most LÖVE functions return values and expect arguments in terms of pixels rather than density-independent units.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: A number in density-independent units to convert to pixels.

returns.name: pixelvalue
returns.description: The converted number, in pixels.

variants.description: The units of love.graphics.getWidth, love.graphics.getHeight, love.mouse.getPosition, mouse events, love.touch.getPosition, and touch events are always in terms of pixels.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x-axis value of a coordinate in density-independent units to convert to pixels.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y-axis value of a coordinate in density-independent units to convert to pixels.

returns.name: px
returns.description: The converted x-axis value of the coordinate, in pixels.

returns.name: py
returns.description: The converted y-axis value of the coordinate, in pixels.

entries.name: updateMode
entries.description:
Sets the display mode and properties of the window, without modifying unspecified properties.

If width or height is 0, updateMode will use the width and height of the desktop. 

Changing the display mode may have side effects: for example, canvases will be cleared. Make sure to save the contents of canvases beforehand or re-draw to them afterward if you need to.

variants.description:
If fullscreen is enabled and the width or height is not supported (see resize event will be triggered.

If the fullscreen type is 'desktop', then the window will be automatically resized to the desktop resolution.

Transparent backgrounds are currently not supported.

arguments.name: width
arguments.description: Window width.

arguments.name: height
arguments.description: Window height.

arguments.name: settings
arguments.description: The settings table with the following optional fields. Any field not filled in will use the current value that would be returned by love.window.getMode.

table.name: fullscreen
table.description: Fullscreen (true), or windowed (false).

table.name: fullscreentype
table.description: The type of fullscreen to use.

table.name: vsync
table.description: True if LÖVE should wait for vsync, false otherwise.

table.name: msaa
table.description: The number of antialiasing samples.

table.name: resizable
table.description: True if the window should be resizable in windowed mode, false otherwise.

table.name: borderless
table.description: True if the window should be borderless in windowed mode, false otherwise.

table.name: centered
table.description: True if the window should be centered in windowed mode, false otherwise.

table.name: display
table.description: The index of the display to show the window in, if multiple monitors are available.

table.name: minwidth
table.description: The minimum width of the window, if it's resizable. Cannot be less than 1.

table.name: minheight
table.description: The minimum height of the window, if it's resizable. Cannot be less than 1.

table.name: highdpi
table.description: True if high-dpi mode should be used on Retina displays in macOS and iOS. Does nothing on non-Retina displays.

table.name: x
table.description: The x-coordinate of the window's position in the specified display.

table.name: y
table.description: The y-coordinate of the window's position in the specified display.

returns.name: success
returns.description: True if successful, false otherwise.

entries.name: MatrixLayout
entries.description: The layout of matrix elements (row-major or column-major).

constants.name: row
constants.description: The matrix is row-major:

constants.name: column
constants.description: The matrix is column-major:

entries.name: Mesh
entries.description: A 2D polygon mesh used for drawing arbitrary textured shapes.

entries.name: attachAttribute
entries.description: Attaches a vertex attribute from a different Mesh onto this Mesh, for use when drawing. This can be used to share vertex attribute data between several different Meshes.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the vertex attribute to attach.

arguments.name: mesh
arguments.description: The Mesh to get the vertex attribute from.

variants.description:
If a Mesh wasn't created with a custom vertex format, it will have 3 vertex attributes named VertexPosition, VertexTexCoord, and VertexColor.

Custom named attributes can be accessed in a vertex shader by declaring them as attribute vec4 MyCustomAttributeName; at the top-level of the vertex shader code. The name must match what was specified in the Mesh's vertex format and in the name argument of Mesh:attachAttribute.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the vertex attribute to attach.

arguments.name: mesh
arguments.description: The Mesh to get the vertex attribute from.

arguments.name: step
arguments.description: Whether the attribute will be per-vertex or per-instance when the mesh is drawn.

arguments.name: attachname
arguments.description: The name of the attribute to use in shader code. Defaults to the name of the attribute in the given mesh. Can be used to use a different name for this attribute when rendering.

entries.name: detachAttribute
entries.description: Removes a previously attached vertex attribute from this Mesh.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the attached vertex attribute to detach.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the attribute was successfully detached.

entries.name: flush
entries.description:
Immediately sends all modified vertex data in the Mesh to the graphics card.

Normally it isn't necessary to call this method as love.graphics.draw(mesh, ...) will do it automatically if needed, but explicitly using **Mesh:flush** gives more control over when the work happens.

If this method is used, it generally shouldn't be called more than once (at most) between love.graphics.draw(mesh, ...) calls.

entries.name: getDrawMode
entries.description: Gets the mode used when drawing the Mesh.

returns.name: mode
returns.description: The mode used when drawing the Mesh.

entries.name: getDrawRange
entries.description: Gets the range of vertices used when drawing the Mesh.

variants.description: If the Mesh's draw range has not been set previously with Mesh:setDrawRange, this function will return nil.

returns.name: min
returns.description: The index of the first vertex used when drawing, or the index of the first value in the vertex map used if one is set for this Mesh.

returns.name: max
returns.description: The index of the last vertex used when drawing, or the index of the last value in the vertex map used if one is set for this Mesh.

entries.name: getTexture
entries.description: Gets the texture (Image or Canvas) used when drawing the Mesh.

returns.name: texture
returns.description: The Image or Canvas to texture the Mesh with when drawing, or nil if none is set.

entries.name: getVertex
entries.description:
Gets the properties of a vertex in the Mesh.

In versions prior to 11.0, color and byte component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

variants.description: The values are returned in the same order as the vertex attributes in the Mesh's vertex format. A standard Mesh that wasn't created with a custom vertex format will return two position numbers, two texture coordinate numbers, and four color components: x, y, u, v, r, g, b, a.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The one-based index of the vertex you want to retrieve the information for.

returns.name: attributecomponent
returns.description: The first component of the first vertex attribute in the specified vertex.
Additional components of all vertex attributes in the specified vertex.

variants.description: Gets the vertex components of a Mesh that wasn't created with a custom vertex format.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The index of the vertex you want to retrieve the information for.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The position of the vertex on the x-axis.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The position of the vertex on the y-axis.

returns.name: u
returns.description: The horizontal component of the texture coordinate.

returns.name: v
returns.description: The vertical component of the texture coordinate.

returns.name: r
returns.description: The red component of the vertex's color.

returns.name: g
returns.description: The green component of the vertex's color.

returns.name: b
returns.description: The blue component of the vertex's color.

returns.name: a
returns.description: The alpha component of the vertex's color.

entries.name: getVertexAttribute
entries.description:
Gets the properties of a specific attribute within a vertex in the Mesh.

Meshes without a custom vertex format specified in love.graphics.newMesh have position as their first attribute, texture coordinates as their second attribute, and color as their third attribute.

arguments.name: vertexindex
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex you want to retrieve the attribute for (one-based).

arguments.name: attributeindex
arguments.description: The index of the attribute within the vertex to be retrieved (one-based).

returns.name: value1
returns.description: The value of the first component of the attribute.

returns.name: value2
returns.description: The value of the second component of the attribute.
Any additional vertex attribute components.

entries.name: getVertexCount
entries.description: Gets the total number of vertices in the Mesh.

returns.name: count
returns.description: The total number of vertices in the mesh.

entries.name: getVertexFormat
entries.description: Gets the vertex format that the Mesh was created with.

returns.name: format
returns.description: The vertex format of the Mesh, which is a table containing tables for each vertex attribute the Mesh was created with, in the form of {attribute, ...}.

table.name: attribute
table.description: A table containing the attribute's name, it's data type, and the number of components in the attribute, in the form of {name, datatype, components}.
Additional vertex attributes in the Mesh.

entries.name: getVertexMap
entries.description:
Gets the vertex map for the Mesh. The vertex map describes the order in which the vertices are used when the Mesh is drawn. The vertices, vertex map, and mesh draw mode work together to determine what exactly is displayed on the screen.

If no vertex map has been set previously via Mesh:setVertexMap, then this function will return nil in LÖVE 0.10.0+, or an empty table in 0.9.2 and older.

returns.name: map
returns.description: A table containing the list of vertex indices used when drawing.

entries.name: isAttributeEnabled
entries.description: Gets whether a specific vertex attribute in the Mesh is enabled. Vertex data from disabled attributes is not used when drawing the Mesh.

variants.description:
If a Mesh wasn't created with a custom vertex format, it will have 3 vertex attributes named VertexPosition, VertexTexCoord, and VertexColor. Otherwise the attribute name must either match one of the vertex attributes specified in the vertex format when creating the Mesh, 

or must match a vertex attribute from another Mesh attached to this Mesh via Mesh:attachAttribute.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the vertex attribute to be checked.

returns.name: enabled
returns.description: Whether the vertex attribute is used when drawing this Mesh.

entries.name: setAttributeEnabled
entries.description: Enables or disables a specific vertex attribute in the Mesh. Vertex data from disabled attributes is not used when drawing the Mesh.

variants.description:
If a Mesh wasn't created with a custom vertex format, it will have 3 vertex attributes named VertexPosition, VertexTexCoord, and VertexColor. Otherwise the attribute name must either match one of the vertex attributes specified in the vertex format when creating the Mesh, 

or must match a vertex attribute from another Mesh attached to this Mesh via Mesh:attachAttribute.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the vertex attribute to enable or disable.

arguments.name: enable
arguments.description: Whether the vertex attribute is used when drawing this Mesh.

entries.name: setDrawMode
entries.description: Sets the mode used when drawing the Mesh.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: The mode to use when drawing the Mesh.

entries.name: setDrawRange
entries.description: Restricts the drawn vertices of the Mesh to a subset of the total.

arguments.name: start
arguments.description: The index of the first vertex to use when drawing, or the index of the first value in the vertex map to use if one is set for this Mesh.

arguments.name: count
arguments.description: The number of vertices to use when drawing, or number of values in the vertex map to use if one is set for this Mesh.

variants.description: Allows all vertices in the Mesh to be drawn.

entries.name: setTexture
entries.description: Sets the texture (Image or Canvas) used when drawing the Mesh.

arguments.name: texture
arguments.description: The Image or Canvas to texture the Mesh with when drawing.

variants.description: Disables any texture from being used when drawing the Mesh. Untextured meshes have a white color by default.

entries.name: setVertex
entries.description:
Sets the properties of a vertex in the Mesh.

In versions prior to 11.0, color and byte component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

variants.description:
The arguments are in the same order as the vertex attributes in the Mesh's vertex format. A standard Mesh that wasn't created with a custom vertex format will use two position numbers, two texture coordinate numbers, and four color components per vertex: x, y, u, v, r, g, b, a.

If no value is supplied for a specific vertex attribute component, it will be set to a default value of 0 if its data type is 'float', or 1 if its data type is 'byte'.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex you want to modify (one-based).

arguments.name: attributecomponent
arguments.description: The first component of the first vertex attribute in the specified vertex.
Additional components of all vertex attributes in the specified vertex.

variants.description:
The table indices are in the same order as the vertex attributes in the Mesh's vertex format. A standard Mesh that wasn't created with a custom vertex format will use two position numbers, two texture coordinate numbers, and four color components per vertex: x, y, u, v, r, g, b, a.

If no value is supplied for a specific vertex attribute component, it will be set to a default value of 0 if its data type is 'float', or 1 if its data type is 'byte'.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex you want to modify (one-based).

arguments.name: vertex
arguments.description: A table with vertex information, in the form of {attributecomponent, ...}.

table.name: attributecomponent
table.description: The first component of the first vertex attribute in the specified vertex.
Additional components of all vertex attributes in the specified vertex.

variants.description: Sets the vertex components of a Mesh that wasn't created with a custom vertex format.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex you want to modify (one-based).

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The position of the vertex on the x-axis.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The position of the vertex on the y-axis.

arguments.name: u
arguments.description: The horizontal component of the texture coordinate.

arguments.name: v
arguments.description: The vertical component of the texture coordinate.

arguments.name: r
arguments.description: The red component of the vertex's color.

arguments.name: g
arguments.description: The green component of the vertex's color.

arguments.name: b
arguments.description: The blue component of the vertex's color.

arguments.name: a
arguments.description: The alpha component of the vertex's color.

variants.description: Sets the vertex components of a Mesh that wasn't created with a custom vertex format.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex you want to modify (one-based).

arguments.name: vertex
arguments.description: A table with vertex information.

table.description: The position of the vertex on the x-axis.
The position of the vertex on the y-axis.
The u texture coordinate. Texture coordinates are normally in the range of 1, but can be greater or less (see WrapMode.)
The v texture coordinate. Texture coordinates are normally in the range of 1, but can be greater or less (see WrapMode.)
The red color component.
The green color component.
The blue color component.
The alpha color component.

entries.name: setVertexAttribute
entries.description:
Sets the properties of a specific attribute within a vertex in the Mesh.

Meshes without a custom vertex format specified in love.graphics.newMesh have position as their first attribute, texture coordinates as their second attribute, and color as their third attribute.

variants.description: Attribute components which exist within the attribute but are not specified as arguments default to 0 for attributes with the float data type, and 255 for the byte data type.

arguments.name: vertexindex
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex to be modified (one-based).

arguments.name: attributeindex
arguments.description: The index of the attribute within the vertex to be modified (one-based).

arguments.name: value1
arguments.description: The new value for the first component of the attribute.

arguments.name: value2
arguments.description: The new value for the second component of the attribute.
Any additional vertex attribute components.

entries.name: setVertexMap
entries.description:
Sets the vertex map for the Mesh. The vertex map describes the order in which the vertices are used when the Mesh is drawn. The vertices, vertex map, and mesh draw mode work together to determine what exactly is displayed on the screen.

The vertex map allows you to re-order or reuse vertices when drawing without changing the actual vertex parameters or duplicating vertices. It is especially useful when combined with different Mesh Draw Modes.

arguments.name: map
arguments.description: A table containing a list of vertex indices to use when drawing. Values must be in the range of Mesh:getVertexCount().

arguments.name: vi1
arguments.description: The index of the first vertex to use when drawing. Must be in the range of Mesh:getVertexCount().

arguments.name: vi2
arguments.description: The index of the second vertex to use when drawing.

arguments.name: vi3
arguments.description: The index of the third vertex to use when drawing.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: Array of vertex indices to use when drawing. Values must be in the range of Mesh:getVertexCount()-1

arguments.name: datatype
arguments.description: Datatype of the vertex indices array above.

entries.name: setVertices
entries.description: Replaces a range of vertices in the Mesh with new ones. The total number of vertices in a Mesh cannot be changed after it has been created. This is often more efficient than calling Mesh:setVertex in a loop.

variants.description:
The values in each vertex table are in the same order as the vertex attributes in the Mesh's vertex format. A standard Mesh that wasn't created with a custom vertex format will use two position numbers, two texture coordinate numbers, and four color components per vertex: x, y, u, v, r, g, b, a.

If no value is supplied for a specific vertex attribute component, it will be set to a default value of 0 if its data type is 'float', or 255 if its data type is 'byte'.

arguments.name: vertices
arguments.description: The table filled with vertex information tables for each vertex, in the form of {vertex, ...} where each vertex is a table in the form of {attributecomponent, ...}.

table.name: attributecomponent
table.description: The first component of the first vertex attribute in the vertex.
Additional components of all vertex attributes in the vertex.

arguments.name: startvertex
arguments.description: The index of the first vertex to replace.

arguments.name: count
arguments.description: Amount of vertices to replace.

variants.description:
Sets the vertex components of the Mesh by copying directly from the memory of a Data object.

If LuaJIT's FFI is used to populate the Data object via Data:getPointer and ffi.cast, this variant can be drastically more efficient than other methods of setting Mesh vertex data.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: A Data object to copy from. The contents of the Data must match the layout of this Mesh's vertex format.

arguments.name: startvertex
arguments.description: The index of the first vertex to replace.

variants.description: Sets the vertex components of a Mesh that wasn't created with a custom vertex format.

arguments.name: vertices
arguments.description: The table filled with vertex information tables for each vertex as follows:

table.description: The position of the vertex on the x-axis.
The position of the vertex on the y-axis.
The horizontal component of the texture coordinate. Texture coordinates are normally in the range of 1, but can be greater or less (see WrapMode).
The vertical component of the texture coordinate. Texture coordinates are normally in the range of 1, but can be greater or less (see WrapMode).
The red color component.
The green color component.
The blue color component.
The alpha color component.

entries.name: MeshDrawMode
entries.description: How a Mesh's vertices are used when drawing.

constants.name: fan
constants.description: The vertices create a "fan" shape with the first vertex acting as the hub point. Can be easily used to draw simple convex polygons.

constants.name: strip
constants.description: The vertices create a series of connected triangles using vertices 1, 2, 3, then 3, 2, 4 (note the order), then 3, 4, 5, and so on.

constants.name: triangles
constants.description: The vertices create unconnected triangles.

constants.name: points
constants.description: The vertices are drawn as unconnected points (see love.graphics.setPointSize.)

entries.name: MessageBoxType
entries.description: Types of message box dialogs. Different types may have slightly different looks.

constants.name: info
constants.description: Informational dialog.

constants.name: warning
constants.description: Warning dialog.

constants.name: error
constants.description: Error dialog.

entries.name: MipmapMode
entries.description: Controls whether a Canvas has mipmaps, and its behaviour when it does.

constants.name: none
constants.description: The Canvas has no mipmaps.

constants.name: auto
constants.description: The Canvas has mipmaps. love.graphics.setCanvas can be used to render to a specific mipmap level, or Canvas:generateMipmaps can (re-)compute all mipmap levels based on the base level.

constants.name: manual
constants.description: The Canvas has mipmaps, and all mipmap levels will automatically be recomputed when switching away from the Canvas with love.graphics.setCanvas.

entries.name: MotorJoint
entries.description: Controls the relative motion between two Bodies. Position and rotation offsets can be specified, as well as the maximum motor force and torque that will be applied to reach the target offsets.

entries.name: getAngularOffset
entries.description: Gets the target angular offset between the two Bodies the Joint is attached to.

returns.name: angleoffset
returns.description: The target angular offset in radians: the second body's angle minus the first body's angle.

entries.name: getLinearOffset
entries.description: Gets the target linear offset between the two Bodies the Joint is attached to.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The x component of the target linear offset, relative to the first Body.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The y component of the target linear offset, relative to the first Body.

entries.name: setAngularOffset
entries.description: Sets the target angluar offset between the two Bodies the Joint is attached to.

arguments.name: angleoffset
arguments.description: The target angular offset in radians: the second body's angle minus the first body's angle.

entries.name: setLinearOffset
entries.description: Sets the target linear offset between the two Bodies the Joint is attached to.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x component of the target linear offset, relative to the first Body.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y component of the target linear offset, relative to the first Body.

entries.name: MouseJoint
entries.description: For controlling objects with the mouse.

entries.name: getDampingRatio
entries.description: Returns the damping ratio.

returns.name: ratio
returns.description: The new damping ratio.

entries.name: getFrequency
entries.description: Returns the frequency.

returns.name: freq
returns.description: The frequency in hertz.

entries.name: getMaxForce
entries.description: Gets the highest allowed force.

returns.name: f
returns.description: The max allowed force.

entries.name: getTarget
entries.description: Gets the target point.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The x-component of the target.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The x-component of the target.

entries.name: setDampingRatio
entries.description: Sets a new damping ratio.

arguments.name: ratio
arguments.description: The new damping ratio.

entries.name: setFrequency
entries.description: Sets a new frequency.

arguments.name: freq
arguments.description: The new frequency in hertz.

entries.name: setMaxForce
entries.description: Sets the highest allowed force.

arguments.name: f
arguments.description: The max allowed force.

entries.name: setTarget
entries.description: Sets the target point.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x-component of the target.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y-component of the target.

entries.name: Object
entries.description: The superclass of all LÖVE types.

entries.name: release
entries.description:
Destroys the object's Lua reference. The object will be completely deleted if it's not referenced by any other LÖVE object or thread.

This method can be used to immediately clean up resources without waiting for Lua's garbage collector.

returns.name: success
returns.description: True if the object was released by this call, false if it had been previously released.

entries.name: type
entries.description: Gets the type of the object as a string.

returns.name: type
returns.description: The type as a string.

entries.name: typeOf
entries.description: Checks whether an object is of a certain type. If the object has the type with the specified name in its hierarchy, this function will return true.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the type to check for.

returns.name: b
returns.description: True if the object is of the specified type, false otherwise.

entries.name: ParticleInsertMode
entries.description: How newly created particles are added to the ParticleSystem.

constants.name: top
constants.description: Particles are inserted at the top of the ParticleSystem's list of particles.

constants.name: bottom
constants.description: Particles are inserted at the bottom of the ParticleSystem's list of particles.

constants.name: random
constants.description: Particles are inserted at random positions in the ParticleSystem's list of particles.

entries.name: ParticleSystem
entries.description:
A ParticleSystem can be used to create particle effects like fire or smoke.

The particle system has to be created using update it in the update callback to see any changes in the particles emitted.

The particle system won't create any particles unless you call setParticleLifetime and setEmissionRate.

entries.name: clone
entries.description: Creates an identical copy of the ParticleSystem in the stopped state.

variants.description: Cloned ParticleSystem inherit all the set-able state of the original ParticleSystem, but they are initialized stopped.

returns.name: particlesystem
returns.description: The new identical copy of this ParticleSystem.

entries.name: emit
entries.description: Emits a burst of particles from the particle emitter.

arguments.name: numparticles
arguments.description: The amount of particles to emit. The number of emitted particles will be truncated if the particle system's max buffer size is reached.

entries.name: getBufferSize
entries.description: Gets the maximum number of particles the ParticleSystem can have at once.

returns.name: size
returns.description: The maximum number of particles.

entries.name: getColors
entries.description:
Gets the series of colors applied to the particle sprite.

In versions prior to 11.0, color component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

returns.name: r1
returns.description: First color, red component (0-1).

returns.name: g1
returns.description: First color, green component (0-1).

returns.name: b1
returns.description: First color, blue component (0-1).

returns.name: a1
returns.description: First color, alpha component (0-1).

returns.name: r2
returns.description: Second color, red component (0-1).

returns.name: g2
returns.description: Second color, green component (0-1).

returns.name: b2
returns.description: Second color, blue component (0-1).

returns.name: a2
returns.description: Second color, alpha component (0-1).

returns.name: r8
returns.description: Eighth color, red component (0-1).

returns.name: g8
returns.description: Eighth color, green component (0-1).

returns.name: b8
returns.description: Eighth color, blue component (0-1).

returns.name: a8
returns.description: Eighth color, alpha component (0-1).

entries.name: getCount
entries.description: Gets the number of particles that are currently in the system.

returns.name: count
returns.description: The current number of live particles.

entries.name: getDirection
entries.description: Gets the direction of the particle emitter (in radians).

returns.name: direction
returns.description: The direction of the emitter (radians).

entries.name: getEmissionArea
entries.description: Gets the area-based spawn parameters for the particles.

returns.name: distribution
returns.description: The type of distribution for new particles.

returns.name: dx
returns.description: The maximum spawn distance from the emitter along the x-axis for uniform distribution, or the standard deviation along the x-axis for normal distribution.

returns.name: dy
returns.description: The maximum spawn distance from the emitter along the y-axis for uniform distribution, or the standard deviation along the y-axis for normal distribution.

returns.name: angle
returns.description: The angle in radians of the emission area.

returns.name: directionRelativeToCenter
returns.description: True if newly spawned particles will be oriented relative to the center of the emission area, false otherwise.

entries.name: getEmissionRate
entries.description: Gets the amount of particles emitted per second.

returns.name: rate
returns.description: The amount of particles per second.

entries.name: getEmitterLifetime
entries.description: Gets how long the particle system will emit particles (if -1 then it emits particles forever).

returns.name: life
returns.description: The lifetime of the emitter (in seconds).

entries.name: getInsertMode
entries.description: Gets the mode used when the ParticleSystem adds new particles.

returns.name: mode
returns.description: The mode used when the ParticleSystem adds new particles.

entries.name: getLinearAcceleration
entries.description:
Gets the linear acceleration (acceleration along the x and y axes) for particles.

Every particle created will accelerate along the x and y axes between xmin,ymin and xmax,ymax.

returns.description: The minimum acceleration along the x axis.

returns.name: ymin

returns.name: xmax
returns.description: The maximum acceleration along the x axis.

returns.name: ymax
returns.description: The maximum acceleration along the y axis.

entries.description: Gets the amount of linear damping (constant deceleration) for particles.

returns.name: min
returns.description: The minimum amount of linear damping applied to particles.

returns.name: max
returns.description: The maximum amount of linear damping applied to particles.

entries.description: Gets the lifetime of the particles.

returns.description: The minimum life of the particles (in seconds).

returns.name: max
returns.description: The maximum life of the particles (in seconds).

returns.name: max

returns.name: max

entries.name: getSizes

entries.description: Gets whether particle angles and rotations are relative to their velocities. If enabled, particles are aligned to the angle of their velocities and rotate relative to that angle.

returns.name: enable
returns.description: True if relative particle rotation is enabled, false if it's disabled.

entries.description: Checks whether the particle system is actively emitting particles.

returns.description: True if system is active, false otherwise.

entries.name: isPaused
entries.description: Checks whether the particle system is paused.

returns.name: paused
returns.description: True if system is paused, false otherwise.

entries.name: isStopped
entries.description: Checks whether the particle system is stopped.

returns.name: stopped
returns.description: True if system is stopped, false otherwise.

entries.name: moveTo
entries.description: Moves the position of the emitter. This results in smoother particle spawning behaviour than if ParticleSystem:setPosition is used every frame.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: Position along x-axis.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Position along y-axis.

entries.name: pause
entries.description: Pauses the particle emitter.

entries.name: reset
entries.description: Resets the particle emitter, removing any existing particles and resetting the lifetime counter.

entries.name: setBufferSize
entries.description: Sets the size of the buffer (the max allowed amount of particles in the system).

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: The buffer size.

entries.name: setColors
entries.description:
Sets a series of colors to apply to the particle sprite. The particle system will interpolate between each color evenly over the particle's lifetime.

Arguments can be passed in groups of four, representing the components of the desired RGBA value, or as tables of RGBA component values, with a default alpha value of 1 if only three values are given. At least one color must be specified. A maximum of eight may be used.

In versions prior to 11.0, color component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

arguments.name: r1
arguments.description: First color, red component (0-1).

arguments.name: g1
arguments.description: First color, green component (0-1).

arguments.name: b1
arguments.description: First color, blue component (0-1).

arguments.name: a1
arguments.description: First color, alpha component (0-1).
Additional colors.

arguments.name: rgba1
arguments.description: First color, a numerical indexed table with the red, green, blue and alpha values as numbers (0-1). The alpha is optional and defaults to 1 if it is left out.
Additional color, a numerical indexed table with the red, green, blue and alpha values as numbers (0-1). The alpha is optional and defaults to 1 if it is left out.

entries.name: setDirection
entries.description: Sets the direction the particles will be emitted in.

arguments.name: direction
arguments.description: The direction of the particles (in radians).

entries.name: setEmissionArea
entries.description: Sets area-based spawn parameters for the particles. Newly created particles will spawn in an area around the emitter based on the parameters to this function.

arguments.name: distribution
arguments.description: The type of distribution for new particles.

arguments.name: dx
arguments.description: The maximum spawn distance from the emitter along the x-axis for uniform distribution, or the standard deviation along the x-axis for normal distribution.

arguments.name: dy
arguments.description: The maximum spawn distance from the emitter along the y-axis for uniform distribution, or the standard deviation along the y-axis for normal distribution.

arguments.name: angle
arguments.description: The angle in radians of the emission area.

arguments.name: directionRelativeToCenter
arguments.description: True if newly spawned particles will be oriented relative to the center of the emission area, false otherwise.

entries.name: setEmissionRate
entries.description: Sets the amount of particles emitted per second.

arguments.name: rate
arguments.description: The amount of particles per second.

entries.description: Sets how long the particle system should emit particles (if -1 then it emits particles forever).

arguments.name: life
arguments.description: The lifetime of the emitter (in seconds).

entries.description: Sets the mode to use when the ParticleSystem adds new particles.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: The mode to use when the ParticleSystem adds new particles.

Sets the linear acceleration (acceleration along the x and y axes) for particles.

Every particle created will accelerate along the x and y axes between xmin,ymin and xmax,ymax.

arguments.name: ymin

Set the offset position which the particle sprite is rotated around.

If this function is not used, the particles rotate around their center.

entries.description: Sets the lifetime of the particles.

arguments.name: max
arguments.description: The maximum life of the particles (in seconds).

entries.description: Sets the position of the emitter.

Additional Quads to use.
