entries.name: PixelFormat
entries.description: Pixel formats for Textures, ImageData, and CompressedImageData.

constants.name: unknown
constants.description: Indicates unknown pixel format, used internally.

constants.name: normal
constants.description: Alias for rgba8, or srgba8 if gamma-correct rendering is enabled.

constants.name: hdr
constants.description: A format suitable for high dynamic range content - an alias for the rgba16f format, normally.

constants.name: r8
constants.description: Single-channel (red component) format (8 bpp).

constants.name: rg8
constants.description: Two channels (red and green components) with 8 bits per channel (16 bpp).

constants.name: rgba8
constants.description: 8 bits per channel (32 bpp) RGBA. Color channel values range from 0-255 (0-1 in shaders).

constants.name: srgba8
constants.description: gamma-correct version of rgba8.

constants.name: r16
constants.description: Single-channel (red component) format (16 bpp).

constants.name: rg16
constants.description: Two channels (red and green components) with 16 bits per channel (32 bpp).

constants.name: rgba16
constants.description: 16 bits per channel (64 bpp) RGBA. Color channel values range from 0-65535 (0-1 in shaders).

constants.name: r16f
constants.description: Floating point single-channel format (16 bpp). Color values can range from [-65504, +65504].

constants.name: rg16f
constants.description: Floating point two-channel format with 16 bits per channel (32 bpp). Color values can range from [-65504, +65504].

constants.name: rgba16f
constants.description: Floating point RGBA with 16 bits per channel (64 bpp). Color values can range from [-65504, +65504].

constants.name: r32f
constants.description: Floating point single-channel format (32 bpp).

constants.name: rg32f
constants.description: Floating point two-channel format with 32 bits per channel (64 bpp).

constants.name: rgba32f
constants.description: Floating point RGBA with 32 bits per channel (128 bpp).

constants.name: la8
constants.description: Same as rg8, but accessed as (L, L, L, A)

constants.name: rgba4
constants.description: 4 bits per channel (16 bpp) RGBA.

constants.name: rgb5a1
constants.description: RGB with 5 bits each, and a 1-bit alpha channel (16 bpp).

constants.name: rgb565
constants.description: RGB with 5, 6, and 5 bits each, respectively (16 bpp). There is no alpha channel in this format.

constants.name: rgb10a2
constants.description: RGB with 10 bits per channel, and a 2-bit alpha channel (32 bpp).

constants.name: rg11b10f
constants.description: Floating point RGB with 11 bits in the red and green channels, and 10 bits in the blue channel (32 bpp). There is no alpha channel. Color values can range from [0, +65024].

constants.name: stencil8
constants.description: No depth buffer and 8-bit stencil buffer.

constants.name: depth16
constants.description: 16-bit depth buffer and no stencil buffer.

constants.name: depth24
constants.description: 24-bit depth buffer and no stencil buffer.

constants.name: depth32f
constants.description: 32-bit float depth buffer and no stencil buffer.

constants.name: depth24stencil8
constants.description: 24-bit depth buffer and 8-bit stencil buffer.

constants.name: depth32fstencil8
constants.description: 32-bit float depth buffer and 8-bit stencil buffer.

constants.name: DXT1
constants.description: The DXT1 format. RGB data at 4 bits per pixel (compared to 32 bits for ImageData and regular Images.) Suitable for fully opaque images on desktop systems.

constants.name: DXT3
constants.description: The DXT3 format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel. Smooth variations in opacity do not mix well with this format.

constants.name: DXT5
constants.description: The DXT5 format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel. Recommended for images with varying opacity on desktop systems.

constants.name: BC4
constants.description: The BC4 format (also known as 3Dc+ or ATI1.) Stores just the red channel, at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: BC4s
constants.description: The signed variant of the BC4 format. Same as above but pixel values in the texture are in the range of 1 instead of 1 in shaders.

constants.name: BC5
constants.description: The BC5 format (also known as 3Dc or ATI2.) Stores red and green channels at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: BC5s
constants.description: The signed variant of the BC5 format.

constants.name: BC6h
constants.description: The BC6H format. Stores half-precision floating-point RGB data in the range of 65504 at 8 bits per pixel. Suitable for HDR images on desktop systems.

constants.name: BC6hs
constants.description: The signed variant of the BC6H format. Stores RGB data in the range of +65504.

constants.name: BC7
constants.description: The BC7 format (also known as BPTC.) Stores RGB or RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ETC1
constants.description: The ETC1 format. RGB data at 4 bits per pixel. Suitable for fully opaque images on older Android devices.

constants.name: ETC2rgb
constants.description: The RGB variant of the ETC2 format. RGB data at 4 bits per pixel. Suitable for fully opaque images on newer mobile devices.

constants.name: ETC2rgba
constants.description: The RGBA variant of the ETC2 format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel. Recommended for images with varying opacity on newer mobile devices.

constants.name: ETC2rgba1
constants.description: The RGBA variant of the ETC2 format where pixels are either fully transparent or fully opaque. RGBA data at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: EACr
constants.description: The single-channel variant of the EAC format. Stores just the red channel, at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: EACrs
constants.description: The signed single-channel variant of the EAC format. Same as above but pixel values in the texture are in the range of 1 instead of 1 in shaders.

constants.name: EACrg
constants.description: The two-channel variant of the EAC format. Stores red and green channels at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: EACrgs
constants.description: The signed two-channel variant of the EAC format.

constants.name: PVR1rgb2
constants.description: The 2 bit per pixel RGB variant of the PVRTC1 format. Stores RGB data at 2 bits per pixel. Textures compressed with PVRTC1 formats must be square and power-of-two sized.

constants.name: PVR1rgb4
constants.description: The 4 bit per pixel RGB variant of the PVRTC1 format. Stores RGB data at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: PVR1rgba2
constants.description: The 2 bit per pixel RGBA variant of the PVRTC1 format.

constants.name: PVR1rgba4
constants.description: The 4 bit per pixel RGBA variant of the PVRTC1 format.

constants.name: ASTC4x4
constants.description: The 4x4 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC5x4
constants.description: The 5x4 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 6.4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC5x5
constants.description: The 5x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 5.12 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC6x5
constants.description: The 6x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 4.27 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC6x6
constants.description: The 6x6 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 3.56 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC8x5
constants.description: The 8x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 3.2 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC8x6
constants.description: The 8x6 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2.67 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC8x8
constants.description: The 8x8 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC10x5
constants.description: The 10x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2.56 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC10x6
constants.description: The 10x6 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2.13 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC10x8
constants.description: The 10x8 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 1.6 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC10x10
constants.description: The 10x10 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 1.28 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC12x10
constants.description: The 12x10 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 1.07 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC12x12
constants.description: The 12x12 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 0.89 bits per pixel.

entries.name: PolygonShape
entries.description: A PolygonShape is a convex polygon with up to 8 vertices.

entries.name: getPoints
entries.description:
Get the local coordinates of the polygon's vertices.

This function has a variable number of return values. It can be used in a nested fashion with love.graphics.polygon.

returns.name: x1
returns.description: The x-component of the first vertex.

returns.name: y1
returns.description: The y-component of the first vertex.

returns.name: x2
returns.description: The x-component of the second vertex.

returns.name: y2
returns.description: The y-component of the second vertex.

entries.name: PowerState
entries.description: The basic state of the system's power supply.

constants.name: unknown
constants.description: Cannot determine power status.

constants.name: battery
constants.description: Not plugged in, running on a battery.

constants.name: nobattery
constants.description: Plugged in, no battery available.

constants.name: charging
constants.description: Plugged in, charging battery.

constants.name: charged
constants.description: Plugged in, battery is fully charged.

entries.name: Quad

A quadrilateral (a polygon with four sides and four corners) with texture coordinate information.

Quads can be used to select part of a texture to draw. In this way, one large texture atlas can be loaded, and then split up into sub-images.

entries.name: RandomGenerator
entries.description: A random number generation object which has its own random state.

entries.name: getSeed
entries.description:
Gets the seed of the random number generator object.

The seed is split into two numbers due to Lua's use of doubles for all number values - doubles can't accurately represent integer  values above 2^53, but the seed value is an integer number in the range of 2^64 - 1.

returns.name: low
returns.description: Integer number representing the lower 32 bits of the RandomGenerator's 64 bit seed value.

returns.name: high
returns.description: Integer number representing the higher 32 bits of the RandomGenerator's 64 bit seed value.

entries.name: getState
entries.description:
Gets the current state of the random number generator. This returns an opaque string which is only useful for later use with RandomGenerator:setState in the same major version of LÖVE.

This is different from RandomGenerator:getSeed in that getState gets the RandomGenerator's current state, whereas getSeed gets the previously set seed number.

variants.description: The value of the state string does not depend on the current operating system.

returns.name: state
returns.description: The current state of the RandomGenerator object, represented as a string.

entries.name: random
entries.description: Generates a pseudo-random number in a platform independent manner.

variants.description: Get uniformly distributed pseudo-random number within 1.

returns.name: number
returns.description: The pseudo-random number.

variants.description: Get uniformly distributed pseudo-random integer number within max.

arguments.name: max
arguments.description: The maximum possible value it should return.

returns.name: number
returns.description: The pseudo-random integer number.

variants.description: Get uniformly distributed pseudo-random integer number within max.

arguments.name: min
arguments.description: The minimum possible value it should return.

arguments.name: max
arguments.description: The maximum possible value it should return.

returns.name: number
returns.description: The pseudo-random integer number.

entries.name: randomNormal
entries.description: Get a normally distributed pseudo random number.

arguments.name: stddev
arguments.description: Standard deviation of the distribution.

arguments.name: mean
arguments.description: The mean of the distribution.

returns.name: number
returns.description: Normally distributed random number with variance (stddev)² and the specified mean.

entries.name: setSeed
entries.description: Sets the seed of the random number generator using the specified integer number.

variants.description: Due to Lua's use of double-precision floating point numbers, values above 2^53 cannot be accurately represented. Use the other variant of this function if your seed will have a larger value.

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: The integer number with which you want to seed the randomization. Must be within the range of 2^53.

variants.description: Combines two 32-bit integer numbers into a 64-bit integer value and sets the seed of the random number generator using the value.

arguments.name: low
arguments.description: The lower 32 bits of the seed value. Must be within the range of 2^32 - 1.

arguments.name: high
arguments.description: The higher 32 bits of the seed value. Must be within the range of 2^32 - 1.

entries.name: setState
entries.description:
Sets the current state of the random number generator. The value used as an argument for this function is an opaque string and should only originate from a previous call to RandomGenerator:getState in the same major version of LÖVE.

This is different from RandomGenerator:setSeed in that setState directly sets the RandomGenerator's current implementation-dependent state, whereas setSeed gives it a new seed value.

variants.description: The effect of the state string does not depend on the current operating system.

arguments.name: state
arguments.description: The new state of the RandomGenerator object, represented as a string. This should originate from a previous call to RandomGenerator:getState.

entries.name: RecordingDevice
entries.description: Represents an audio input device capable of recording sounds.

entries.name: getBitDepth
entries.description: Gets the number of bits per sample in the data currently being recorded.

returns.name: bits
returns.description: The number of bits per sample in the data that's currently being recorded.

entries.name: getChannelCount
entries.description: Gets the number of channels currently being recorded (mono or stereo).

returns.name: channels
returns.description: The number of channels being recorded (1 for mono, 2 for stereo).

entries.name: getData
entries.description:
Gets all recorded audio SoundData stored in the device's internal ring buffer.

The internal ring buffer is cleared when this function is called, so calling it again will only get audio recorded after the previous call. If the device's internal ring buffer completely fills up before getData is called, the oldest data that doesn't fit into the buffer will be lost.

returns.name: data
returns.description: The recorded audio data, or nil if the device isn't recording.

entries.name: getName
entries.description: Gets the name of the recording device.

returns.name: name
returns.description: The name of the device.

entries.name: getSampleCount
entries.description: Gets the number of currently recorded samples.

returns.name: samples
returns.description: The number of samples that have been recorded so far.

entries.name: getSampleRate
entries.description: Gets the number of samples per second currently being recorded.

returns.name: rate
returns.description: The number of samples being recorded per second (sample rate).

entries.name: isRecording
entries.description: Gets whether the device is currently recording.

returns.name: recording
returns.description: True if the recording, false otherwise.

entries.name: start
entries.description: Begins recording audio using this device.

variants.description: A ring buffer is used internally to store recorded data before RecordingDevice:getData or RecordingDevice:stop are called – the former clears the buffer. If the buffer completely fills up before getData or stop are called, the oldest data that doesn't fit into the buffer will be lost.

arguments.name: samplecount
arguments.description: The maximum number of samples to store in an internal ring buffer when recording. RecordingDevice:getData clears the internal buffer when called.

arguments.name: samplerate
arguments.description: The number of samples per second to store when recording.

arguments.name: bitdepth
arguments.description: The number of bits per sample.

arguments.name: channels
arguments.description: Whether to record in mono or stereo. Most microphones don't support more than 1 channel.

returns.name: success
returns.description: True if the device successfully began recording using the specified parameters, false if not.

entries.name: stop
entries.description: Stops recording audio from this device. Any sound data currently in the device's buffer will be returned.

returns.name: data
returns.description: The sound data currently in the device's buffer, or nil if the device wasn't recording.

entries.name: Scancode
entries.description:
Keyboard scancodes.

Scancodes are keyboard layout-independent, so the scancode "w" will be generated if the key in the same place as the "w" key on an American QWERTY keyboard is pressed, no matter what the key is labelled or what the user's operating system settings are.

Using scancodes, rather than keycodes, is useful because keyboards with layouts differing from the US/UK layout(s) might have keys that generate 'unknown' keycodes, but the scancodes will still be detected. This however would necessitate having a list for each keyboard layout one would choose to support.

One could use textinput or textedited instead, but those only give back the end result of keys used, i.e. you can't get modifiers on their own from it, only the final symbols that were generated.

entries.name: Shader
entries.description:
A Shader is used for advanced hardware-accelerated pixel or vertex manipulation. These effects are written in a language based on GLSL (OpenGL Shading Language) with a few things simplified for easier coding.

Potential uses for shaders include HDR/bloom, motion blur, grayscale/invert/sepia/any kind of color effect, reflection/refraction, distortions, bump mapping, and much more! Here is a collection of basic shaders and good starting point to learn: https://github.com/vrld/moonshine

entries.name: getWarnings
entries.description: Returns any warning and error messages from compiling the shader code. This can be used for debugging your shaders if there's anything the graphics hardware doesn't like.

returns.name: warnings
returns.description: Warning and error messages (if any).

entries.name: hasUniform
entries.description:
Gets whether a uniform / extern variable exists in the Shader.

If a graphics driver's shader compiler determines that a uniform / extern variable doesn't affect the final output of the shader, it may optimize the variable out. This function will return false in that case.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the uniform variable.

returns.name: hasuniform
returns.description: Whether the uniform exists in the shader and affects its final output.

entries.name: send
entries.description:
Sends one or more values to a special (''uniform'') variable inside the shader. Uniform variables have to be marked using the ''uniform'' or ''extern'' keyword, e.g.

uniform float time;  // 'float' is the typical number type used in GLSL shaders.

uniform float varsvec2 light_pos;

uniform vec4 colors[4;

The corresponding send calls would be

shader:send('time', t)

shader:send('vars',a,b)

shader:send('light_pos', {light_x, light_y})

shader:send('colors', {r1, g1, b1, a1},  {r2, g2, b2, a2},  {r3, g3, b3, a3},  {r4, g4, b4, a4})

Uniform / extern variables are read-only in the shader code and remain constant until modified by a Shader:send call. Uniform variables can be accessed in both the Vertex and Pixel components of a shader, as long as the variable is declared in each.

variants.description: Because all numbers in Lua are floating point, in versions prior to 0.10.2 you must use the function Shader:sendInt to send values to uniform int variables in the shader's code.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the number to send to the shader.

arguments.name: number
arguments.description: Number to send to store in the uniform variable.
Additional numbers to send if the uniform variable is an array.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the vector to send to the shader.

arguments.name: vector
arguments.description: Numbers to send to the uniform variable as a vector. The number of elements in the table determines the type of the vector (e.g. two numbers -> vec2). At least two and at most four numbers can be used.
Additional vectors to send if the uniform variable is an array. All vectors need to be of the same size (e.g. only vec3's).

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the matrix to send to the shader.

arguments.name: matrix
arguments.description: 2x2, 3x3, or 4x4 matrix to send to the uniform variable. Using table form: {{a,b,c,d}, {e,f,g,h}, ... } or (since version 0.10.2) {a,b,c,d, e,f,g,h, ...}. The order in 0.10.2 is column-major; starting in 11.0 it's row-major instead.
Additional matrices of the same type as ''matrix'' to store in a uniform array.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the Texture to send to the shader.

arguments.name: texture
arguments.description: Texture (Image or Canvas) to send to the uniform variable.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the boolean to send to the shader.

arguments.name: boolean
arguments.description: Boolean to send to store in the uniform variable.
Additional booleans to send if the uniform variable is an array.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the matrix to send to the shader.

arguments.name: matrixlayout
arguments.description: The layout (row- or column-major) of the matrix.

arguments.name: matrix
arguments.description: 2x2, 3x3, or 4x4 matrix to send to the uniform variable. Using table form: {{a,b,c,d}, {e,f,g,h}, ... } or {a,b,c,d, e,f,g,h, ...}.
Additional matrices of the same type as ''matrix'' to store in a uniform array.

variants.description: Sends uniform values to the Shader sourced from the contents of a Data object. This directly copies the bytes of the data.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the uniform to send to the shader.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: Data object containing the values to send.

arguments.name: offset
arguments.description: Offset in bytes from the start of the Data object.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: Size in bytes of the data to send. If nil, as many bytes as the specified uniform uses will be copied.

variants.description: Sends uniform matrices to the Shader sourced from the contents of a Data object. This directly copies the bytes of the data.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the uniform matrix to send to the shader.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: Data object containing the values to send.

arguments.name: matrixlayout
arguments.description: The layout (row- or column-major) of the matrix in memory.

arguments.name: offset
arguments.description: Offset in bytes from the start of the Data object.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: Size in bytes of the data to send. If nil, as many bytes as the specified uniform uses will be copied.

variants.description: Sends uniform matrices to the Shader sourced from the contents of a Data object. This directly copies the bytes of the data.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the uniform matrix to send to the shader.

arguments.name: matrixlayout
arguments.description: The layout (row- or column-major) of the matrix in memory.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: Data object containing the values to send.

arguments.name: offset
arguments.description: Offset in bytes from the start of the Data object.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: Size in bytes of the data to send. If nil, as many bytes as the specified uniform uses will be copied.

entries.name: sendColor
entries.description:
Sends one or more colors to a special (''extern'' / ''uniform'') vec3 or vec4 variable inside the shader. The color components must be in the range of 1. The colors are gamma-corrected if global gamma-correction is enabled.

Extern variables must be marked using the ''extern'' keyword, e.g.

extern vec4 Color;

The corresponding sendColor call would be

shader:sendColor('Color', {r, g, b, a})

Extern variables can be accessed in both the Vertex and Pixel stages of a shader, as long as the variable is declared in each.

In versions prior to 11.0, color component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the color extern variable to send to in the shader.

arguments.name: color
arguments.description: A table with red, green, blue, and optional alpha color components in the range of 1 to send to the extern as a vector.
Additional colors to send in case the extern is an array. All colors need to be of the same size (e.g. only vec3's).

entries.name: Shape
entries.description:
Shapes are solid 2d geometrical objects which handle the mass and collision of a Body in love.physics.

Shapes are attached to a Body via a Fixture. The Shape object is copied when this happens. 

The Shape's position is relative to the position of the Body it has been attached to.

entries.name: computeAABB
entries.description: Returns the points of the bounding box for the transformed shape.

arguments.name: tx
arguments.description: The translation of the shape on the x-axis.

arguments.name: ty
arguments.description: The translation of the shape on the y-axis.

arguments.name: tr
arguments.description: The shape rotation.

arguments.name: childIndex
arguments.description: The index of the child to compute the bounding box of.

returns.name: topLeftX
returns.description: The x position of the top-left point.

returns.name: topLeftY
returns.description: The y position of the top-left point.

returns.name: bottomRightX
returns.description: The x position of the bottom-right point.

returns.name: bottomRightY
returns.description: The y position of the bottom-right point.

entries.name: computeMass
entries.description: Computes the mass properties for the shape with the specified density.

arguments.name: density
arguments.description: The shape density.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The x postition of the center of mass.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The y postition of the center of mass.

returns.name: mass
returns.description: The mass of the shape.

returns.name: inertia
returns.description: The rotational inertia.

entries.name: getChildCount
entries.description: Returns the number of children the shape has.

returns.name: count
returns.description: The number of children.

entries.name: getRadius
entries.description: Gets the radius of the shape.

returns.name: radius
returns.description: The radius of the shape.

entries.name: getType
entries.description:
Gets a string representing the Shape.

This function can be useful for conditional debug drawing.

returns.name: type
returns.description: The type of the Shape.

entries.name: rayCast
entries.description:
Casts a ray against the shape and returns the surface normal vector and the line position where the ray hit. If the ray missed the shape, nil will be returned. The Shape can be transformed to get it into the desired position.

The ray starts on the first point of the input line and goes towards the second point of the line. The fourth argument is the maximum distance the ray is going to travel as a scale factor of the input line length.

The childIndex parameter is used to specify which child of a parent shape, such as a ChainShape, will be ray casted. For ChainShapes, the index of 1 is the first edge on the chain. Ray casting a parent shape will only test the child specified so if you want to test every shape of the parent, you must loop through all of its children.

The world position of the impact can be calculated by multiplying the line vector with the third return value and adding it to the line starting point.

hitx, hity = x1 + (x2 - x1) * fraction, y1 + (y2 - y1) * fraction

arguments.name: x1
arguments.description: The x position of the input line starting point.

arguments.name: y1
arguments.description: The y position of the input line starting point.

arguments.name: x2
arguments.description: The x position of the input line end point.

arguments.name: y2
arguments.description: The y position of the input line end point.

arguments.name: maxFraction
arguments.description: Ray length parameter.

arguments.name: tx
arguments.description: The translation of the shape on the x-axis.

arguments.name: ty
arguments.description: The translation of the shape on the y-axis.

arguments.name: tr
arguments.description: The shape rotation.

arguments.name: childIndex
arguments.description: The index of the child the ray gets cast against.

returns.name: xn
returns.description: The x component of the normal vector of the edge where the ray hit the shape.

returns.name: yn
returns.description: The y component of the normal vector of the edge where the ray hit the shape.

returns.name: fraction
returns.description: The position on the input line where the intersection happened as a factor of the line length.

entries.name: testPoint
entries.description: This is particularly useful for mouse interaction with the shapes. By looping through all shapes and testing the mouse position with this function, we can find which shapes the mouse touches.

arguments.name: tx
arguments.description: Translates the shape along the x-axis.

arguments.name: ty
arguments.description: Translates the shape along the y-axis.

arguments.name: tr
arguments.description: Rotates the shape.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x-component of the point.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y-component of the point.

returns.name: hit
returns.description: True if inside, false if outside

entries.name: ShapeType
entries.description: The different types of Shapes, as returned by Shape:getType.

constants.name: circle
constants.description: The Shape is a CircleShape.

constants.name: polygon
constants.description: The Shape is a PolygonShape.

constants.name: edge
constants.description: The Shape is a EdgeShape.

constants.name: chain
constants.description: The Shape is a ChainShape.

entries.name: SoundData
entries.description:
Contains raw audio samples.

You can not play SoundData back directly. You must wrap a Source object around it.

entries.name: getBitDepth
entries.description: Returns the number of bits per sample.

returns.name: bitdepth
returns.description: Either 8, or 16.

entries.name: getChannelCount
entries.description: Returns the number of channels in the SoundData.

returns.name: channels
returns.description: 1 for mono, 2 for stereo.

entries.name: getDuration
entries.description: Gets the duration of the sound data.

returns.name: duration
returns.description: The duration of the sound data in seconds.

entries.name: getSample
entries.description: Gets the value of the sample-point at the specified position. For stereo SoundData objects, the data from the left and right channels are interleaved in that order.

arguments.name: i
arguments.description: An integer value specifying the position of the sample (starting at 0).

returns.name: sample
returns.description: The normalized samplepoint (range -1.0 to 1.0).

variants.description: Gets the value of a sample using an explicit sample index instead of interleaving them in the sample position parameter.

arguments.name: i
arguments.description: An integer value specifying the position of the sample (starting at 0).

arguments.name: channel
arguments.description: The index of the channel to get within the given sample.

returns.name: sample
returns.description: The normalized samplepoint (range -1.0 to 1.0).

entries.name: getSampleCount
entries.description: Returns the number of samples per channel of the SoundData.

returns.name: count
returns.description: Total number of samples.

entries.name: getSampleRate
entries.description: Returns the sample rate of the SoundData.

returns.name: rate
returns.description: Number of samples per second.

entries.name: setSample
entries.description: Sets the value of the sample-point at the specified position. For stereo SoundData objects, the data from the left and right channels are interleaved in that order.

arguments.name: i
arguments.description: An integer value specifying the position of the sample (starting at 0).

arguments.name: sample
arguments.description: The normalized samplepoint (range -1.0 to 1.0).

variants.description: Sets the value of a sample using an explicit sample index instead of interleaving them in the sample position parameter.

arguments.name: i
arguments.description: An integer value specifying the position of the sample (starting at 0).

arguments.name: channel
arguments.description: The index of the channel to set within the given sample.

arguments.name: sample
arguments.description: The normalized samplepoint (range -1.0 to 1.0).

entries.name: Source
entries.description:
A Source represents audio you can play back.

You can do interesting things with Sources, like set the volume, pitch, and its position relative to the listener. Please note that positional audio only works for mono (i.e. non-stereo) sources.

The Source controls (play/pause/stop) act according to the following state table.

entries.name: clone
entries.description:
Creates an identical copy of the Source in the stopped state.

Static Sources will use significantly less memory and take much less time to be created if Source:clone is used to create them instead of love.audio.newSource, so this method should be preferred when making multiple Sources which play the same sound.

variants.description: Cloned Sources inherit all the set-able state of the original Source, but they are initialized stopped.

returns.name: source
returns.description: The new identical copy of this Source.

entries.name: getActiveEffects
entries.description: Gets a list of the Source's active effect names.

returns.name: effects
returns.description: A list of the source's active effect names.

entries.name: getAirAbsorption
entries.description:
Gets the amount of air absorption applied to the Source.

By default the value is set to 0 which means that air absorption effects are disabled. A value of 1 will apply high frequency attenuation to the Source at a rate of 0.05 dB per meter.

variants.description: Audio air absorption functionality is not supported on iOS.

returns.name: amount
returns.description: The amount of air absorption applied to the Source.

entries.name: getAttenuationDistances
entries.description: Gets the reference and maximum attenuation distances of the Source. The values, combined with the current DistanceModel, affect how the Source's volume attenuates based on distance from the listener.

returns.name: ref
returns.description: The current reference attenuation distance. If the current DistanceModel is clamped, this is the minimum distance before the Source is no longer attenuated.

returns.name: max
returns.description: The current maximum attenuation distance.

entries.name: getChannelCount
entries.description: Gets the number of channels in the Source. Only 1-channel (mono) Sources can use directional and positional effects.

returns.name: channels
returns.description: 1 for mono, 2 for stereo.

entries.name: getCone
entries.description: Gets the Source's directional volume cones. Together with Source:setDirection, the cone angles allow for the Source's volume to vary depending on its direction.

returns.name: innerAngle
returns.description: The inner angle from the Source's direction, in radians. The Source will play at normal volume if the listener is inside the cone defined by this angle.

returns.name: outerAngle
returns.description: The outer angle from the Source's direction, in radians. The Source will play at a volume between the normal and outer volumes, if the listener is in between the cones defined by the inner and outer angles.

returns.name: outerVolume
returns.description: The Source's volume when the listener is outside both the inner and outer cone angles.

entries.name: getDirection
entries.description: Gets the direction of the Source.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The X part of the direction vector.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The Y part of the direction vector.

returns.name: z
returns.description: The Z part of the direction vector.

entries.name: getDuration
entries.description: Gets the duration of the Source. For streaming Sources it may not always be sample-accurate, and may return -1 if the duration cannot be determined at all.

arguments.name: unit
arguments.description: The time unit for the return value.

returns.name: duration
returns.description: The duration of the Source, or -1 if it cannot be determined.

entries.name: getEffect
entries.description:
Gets the filter settings associated to a specific effect.

This function returns nil if the effect was applied with no filter settings associated to it.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the effect.

arguments.name: filtersettings
arguments.description: An optional empty table that will be filled with the filter settings.

returns.name: filtersettings
returns.description: The settings for the filter associated to this effect, or nil if the effect is not present in this Source or has no filter associated. The table has the following fields:

table.name: volume
table.description: The overall volume of the audio.

table.name: highgain
table.description: Volume of high-frequency audio. Only applies to low-pass and band-pass filters.

table.name: lowgain
table.description: Volume of low-frequency audio. Only applies to high-pass and band-pass filters.

entries.name: getFilter
entries.description: Gets the filter settings currently applied to the Source.

returns.name: settings
returns.description: The filter settings to use for this Source, or nil if the Source has no active filter. The table has the following fields:

table.name: type
table.description: The type of filter to use.

table.name: volume
table.description: The overall volume of the audio.

table.name: highgain
table.description: Volume of high-frequency audio. Only applies to low-pass and band-pass filters.

table.name: lowgain
table.description: Volume of low-frequency audio. Only applies to high-pass and band-pass filters.

entries.name: getFreeBufferCount
entries.description: Gets the number of free buffer slots in a queueable Source. If the queueable Source is playing, this value will increase up to the amount the Source was created with. If the queueable Source is stopped, it will process all of its internal buffers first, in which case this function will always return the amount it was created with.

returns.name: buffers
returns.description: How many more SoundData objects can be queued up.

entries.name: getPitch
entries.description: Gets the current pitch of the Source.

returns.name: pitch
returns.description: The pitch, where 1.0 is normal.

entries.name: getPosition
entries.description: Gets the position of the Source.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The X position of the Source.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The Y position of the Source.

returns.name: z
returns.description: The Z position of the Source.

entries.name: getRolloff
entries.description: Returns the rolloff factor of the source.

returns.name: rolloff
returns.description: The rolloff factor.

entries.name: getType
entries.description: Gets the type of the Source.

returns.name: sourcetype
returns.description: The type of the source.

entries.name: getVelocity
entries.description: Gets the velocity of the Source.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The X part of the velocity vector.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The Y part of the velocity vector.

returns.name: z
returns.description: The Z part of the velocity vector.

entries.name: getVolume
entries.description: Gets the current volume of the Source.

returns.name: volume
returns.description: The volume of the Source, where 1.0 is normal volume.

entries.name: getVolumeLimits
entries.description: Returns the volume limits of the source.

returns.name: min
returns.description: The minimum volume.

returns.name: max
returns.description: The maximum volume.

entries.name: isLooping
entries.description: Returns whether the Source will loop.

returns.name: loop
returns.description: True if the Source will loop, false otherwise.

entries.name: isPlaying
entries.description: Returns whether the Source is playing.

returns.name: playing
returns.description: True if the Source is playing, false otherwise.

entries.name: isRelative
entries.description: Gets whether the Source's position, velocity, direction, and cone angles are relative to the listener.

returns.name: relative
returns.description: True if the position, velocity, direction and cone angles are relative to the listener, false if they're absolute.

entries.name: pause
entries.description: Pauses the Source.

entries.name: play
entries.description: Starts playing the Source.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the Source was able to successfully start playing.

entries.name: queue
entries.description:
Queues SoundData for playback in a queueable Source.

This method requires the Source to be created via love.audio.newQueueableSource.

arguments.name: sounddata
arguments.description: The data to queue. The SoundData's sample rate, bit depth, and channel count must match the Source's.

returns.name: success
returns.description: True if the data was successfully queued for playback, false if there were no available buffers to use for queueing.

entries.name: seek
entries.description: Sets the currently playing position of the Source.

arguments.name: offset
arguments.description: The position to seek to.

arguments.name: unit
arguments.description: The unit of the position value.

entries.name: setAirAbsorption
entries.description:
Sets the amount of air absorption applied to the Source.

By default the value is set to 0 which means that air absorption effects are disabled. A value of 1 will apply high frequency attenuation to the Source at a rate of 0.05 dB per meter.

Air absorption can simulate sound transmission through foggy air, dry air, smoky atmosphere, etc. It can be used to simulate different atmospheric conditions within different locations in an area.

variants.description: Audio air absorption functionality is not supported on iOS.

arguments.name: amount
arguments.description: The amount of air absorption applied to the Source. Must be between 0 and 10.

entries.name: setAttenuationDistances
entries.description:
Sets the reference and maximum attenuation distances of the Source. The parameters, combined with the current DistanceModel, affect how the Source's volume attenuates based on distance.

Distance attenuation is only applicable to Sources based on mono (rather than stereo) audio.

arguments.name: ref
arguments.description: The new reference attenuation distance. If the current DistanceModel is clamped, this is the minimum attenuation distance.

arguments.name: max
arguments.description: The new maximum attenuation distance.

entries.name: setCone
entries.description: Sets the Source's directional volume cones. Together with Source:setDirection, the cone angles allow for the Source's volume to vary depending on its direction.

arguments.name: innerAngle
arguments.description: The inner angle from the Source's direction, in radians. The Source will play at normal volume if the listener is inside the cone defined by this angle.

arguments.name: outerAngle
arguments.description: The outer angle from the Source's direction, in radians. The Source will play at a volume between the normal and outer volumes, if the listener is in between the cones defined by the inner and outer angles.

arguments.name: outerVolume
arguments.description: The Source's volume when the listener is outside both the inner and outer cone angles.

entries.name: setDirection
entries.description: Sets the direction vector of the Source. A zero vector makes the source non-directional.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The X part of the direction vector.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The Y part of the direction vector.

arguments.name: z
arguments.description: The Z part of the direction vector.

entries.name: setEffect
entries.description:
Applies an audio effect to the Source.

The effect must have been previously defined using love.audio.setEffect.

variants.description: Applies the given previously defined effect to this Source.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the effect previously set up with love.audio.setEffect.

arguments.name: enable
arguments.description: If false and the given effect name was previously enabled on this Source, disables the effect.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the effect was successfully applied to this Source.

variants.description:
Applies the given previously defined effect to this Source, and applies a filter to the Source which affects the sound fed into the effect.

Audio effect functionality is not supported on iOS.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the effect previously set up with love.audio.setEffect.

arguments.name: filtersettings
arguments.description: The filter settings to apply prior to the effect, with the following fields:

table.name: type
table.description: The type of filter to use.

table.name: volume
table.description: The overall volume of the audio. Must be between 0 and 1.

table.name: highgain
table.description: Volume of high-frequency audio. Only applies to low-pass and band-pass filters. Must be between 0 and 1.

table.name: lowgain
table.description: Volume of low-frequency audio. Only applies to high-pass and band-pass filters. Must be between 0 and 1.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the effect and filter were successfully applied to this Source.

entries.name: setFilter
entries.description: Sets a low-pass, high-pass, or band-pass filter to apply when playing the Source.

arguments.name: settings
arguments.description: The filter settings to use for this Source, with the following fields:

table.name: type
table.description: The type of filter to use.

table.name: volume
table.description: The overall volume of the audio. Must be between 0 and 1.

table.name: highgain
table.description: Volume of high-frequency audio. Only applies to low-pass and band-pass filters. Must be between 0 and 1.

table.name: lowgain
table.description: Volume of low-frequency audio. Only applies to high-pass and band-pass filters. Must be between 0 and 1.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the filter was successfully applied to the Source.

variants.description: Disables filtering on this Source.

entries.name: setLooping
entries.description: Sets whether the Source should loop.

arguments.name: loop
arguments.description: True if the source should loop, false otherwise.

entries.name: setPitch
entries.description: Sets the pitch of the Source.

arguments.name: pitch
arguments.description: Calculated with regard to 1 being the base pitch. Each reduction by 50 percent equals a pitch shift of -12 semitones (one octave reduction). Each doubling equals a pitch shift of 12 semitones (one octave increase). Zero is not a legal value.

entries.name: setPosition
entries.description: Sets the position of the Source. Please note that this only works for mono (i.e. non-stereo) sound files!

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The X position of the Source.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The Y position of the Source.

arguments.name: z
arguments.description: The Z position of the Source.

entries.name: setRelative
entries.description:
Sets whether the Source's position, velocity, direction, and cone angles are relative to the listener, or absolute.

By default, all sources are absolute and therefore relative to the origin of love's coordinate system 0, 0. Only absolute sources are affected by the position of the listener. Please note that positional audio only works for mono (i.e. non-stereo) sources.

arguments.name: enable
arguments.description: True to make the position, velocity, direction and cone angles relative to the listener, false to make them absolute.

entries.name: setRolloff
entries.description:
Sets the rolloff factor which affects the strength of the used distance attenuation.

Extended information and detailed formulas can be found in the chapter '3.4. Attenuation By Distance' of OpenAL 1.1 specification.

arguments.name: rolloff
arguments.description: The new rolloff factor.

entries.name: setVelocity
entries.description:
Sets the velocity of the Source.

This does '''not''' change the position of the Source, but lets the application know how it has to calculate the doppler effect.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The X part of the velocity vector.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The Y part of the velocity vector.

arguments.name: z
arguments.description: The Z part of the velocity vector.

entries.name: setVolume
entries.description: Sets the current volume of the Source.

arguments.name: volume
arguments.description: The volume for a Source, where 1.0 is normal volume. Volume cannot be raised above 1.0.

entries.name: setVolumeLimits
entries.description: Sets the volume limits of the source. The limits have to be numbers from 0 to 1.

arguments.name: min
arguments.description: The minimum volume.

arguments.name: max
arguments.description: The maximum volume.

entries.name: stop
entries.description: Stops a Source.

entries.name: tell
entries.description: Gets the currently playing position of the Source.

arguments.name: unit
arguments.description: The type of unit for the return value.

returns.name: position
returns.description: The currently playing position of the Source.

entries.name: SourceType
entries.description:
Types of audio sources.

A good rule of thumb is to use stream for music files and static for all short sound effects. Basically, you want to avoid loading large files into memory at once.

constants.name: static
constants.description: The whole audio is decoded.

constants.name: stream
constants.description: The audio is decoded in chunks when needed.

constants.name: queue
constants.description: The audio must be manually queued by the user.

entries.name: SpriteBatch
entries.description:
Using a single image, draw any number of identical copies of the image using a single call to love.graphics.draw(). This can be used, for example, to draw repeating copies of a single background image with high performance.

A SpriteBatch can be even more useful when the underlying image is a texture atlas (a single image file containing many independent images); by adding Quads to the batch, different sub-images from within the atlas can be drawn.

entries.name: add
entries.description: Adds a sprite to the batch. Sprites are drawn in the order they are added.

arguments.description: The position to draw the object (x-axis).

arguments.name: y

arguments.name: r
arguments.description: Orientation (radians).

arguments.name: sx
arguments.description: Scale factor (x-axis).

arguments.name: sy
arguments.description: Scale factor (y-axis).

arguments.name: ox
arguments.description: Origin offset (x-axis).

arguments.name: oy
arguments.description: Origin offset (y-axis).

arguments.name: kx
arguments.description: Shear factor (x-axis).

arguments.name: ky
arguments.description: Shear factor (y-axis).

returns.name: id
returns.description: An identifier for the added sprite.

arguments.name: quad
arguments.description: The Quad to add.

arguments.name: transform
arguments.description: A transform object.

returns.description: The index of the added sprite, for use with SpriteBatch:set or SpriteBatch:setLayer.

variants.description:
Adds a layer of the SpriteBatch's Array Texture using the specified Quad and Transform.

In order to use an Array Texture or other non-2D texture types as the main texture in a custom void effect() variant must be used in the pixel shader, and MainTex must be declared as an ArrayImage or sampler2DArray like so: uniform ArrayImage MainTex;.

arguments.name: layerindex
arguments.description: The index of the layer to use for this sprite.

arguments.name: quad
arguments.description: The subsection of the texture's layer to use when drawing the sprite.

arguments.name: transform
arguments.description: A transform object.

returns.name: spriteindex
returns.description: The index of the added sprite, for use with SpriteBatch:set or SpriteBatch:setLayer.

entries.name: attachAttribute
entries.description:
Attaches a per-vertex attribute from a Mesh onto this SpriteBatch, for use when drawing. This can be combined with a Shader to augment a SpriteBatch with per-vertex or additional per-sprite information instead of just having per-sprite colors.

Each sprite in a SpriteBatch has 4 vertices in the following order: top-left, bottom-left, top-right, bottom-right. The index returned by SpriteBatch:add (and used by SpriteBatch:set) can used to determine the first vertex of a specific sprite with the formula 1 + 4 * ( id - 1 ).

variants.description:
If a created with a custom vertex format, it will have 3 vertex attributes named VertexPosition, VertexTexCoord, and VertexColor. If vertex attributes with those names are attached to the SpriteBatch, it will override the SpriteBatch's sprite positions, texture coordinates, and sprite colors, respectively.

Custom named attributes can be accessed in a vertex shader by declaring them as attribute vec4 MyCustomAttributeName; at the top-level of the vertex shader code. The name must match what was specified in the Mesh's vertex format and in the name argument of SpriteBatch:attachAttribute.

A Mesh must have at least 4 * SpriteBatch:getBufferSize vertices in order to be attachable to a SpriteBatch.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the vertex attribute to attach.

arguments.name: mesh
arguments.description: The Mesh to get the vertex attribute from.

entries.name: clear
entries.description: Removes all sprites from the buffer.

entries.name: flush
entries.description:
Immediately sends all new and modified sprite data in the batch to the graphics card.

Normally it isn't necessary to call this method as love.graphics.draw(spritebatch, ...) will do it automatically if needed, but explicitly using SpriteBatch:flush gives more control over when the work happens.

If this method is used, it generally shouldn't be called more than once (at most) between love.graphics.draw(spritebatch, ...) calls.

entries.name: getBufferSize
entries.description: Gets the maximum number of sprites the SpriteBatch can hold.

returns.name: size
returns.description: The maximum number of sprites the batch can hold.

entries.name: getColor
entries.description:
Gets the color that will be used for the next add and set operations.

If no color has been set with SpriteBatch:setColor or the current SpriteBatch color has been cleared, this method will return nil.

In versions prior to 11.0, color component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

returns.name: r
returns.description: The red component (0-1).

returns.name: g
returns.description: The green component (0-1).

returns.name: b
returns.description: The blue component (0-1).

returns.name: a
returns.description: The alpha component (0-1).

entries.name: getCount
entries.description: Gets the number of sprites currently in the SpriteBatch.

returns.name: count
returns.description: The number of sprites currently in the batch.

entries.name: getTexture
entries.description: Gets the texture (Image or Canvas) used by the SpriteBatch.

returns.name: texture
returns.description: The Image or Canvas used by the SpriteBatch.

entries.description: Changes a sprite in the batch. This requires the sprite index returned by SpriteBatch:add or SpriteBatch:addLayer.

arguments.name: kx

arguments.name: ky
arguments.description: Shear factor (y-axis).

variants.description:
Changes a sprite with a Quad in the batch. This requires the index returned by SpriteBatch:add or SpriteBatch:addLayer.

SpriteBatches do not support removing individual sprites. One can do a pseudo removal (instead of clearing and re-adding everything) by:

SpriteBatch:set(id, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0)

This makes all the sprite's vertices equal (because the x and y scales are 0), which prevents the GPU from fully processing the sprite when drawing the SpriteBatch.

arguments.name: spriteindex
arguments.description: The index of the sprite that will be changed.

arguments.name: quad
arguments.description: The Quad used on the image of the batch.

arguments.name: kx

arguments.name: ky
arguments.description: Shear factor (y-axis).

entries.name: setColor
entries.description:
Sets the color that will be used for the next add and set operations. Calling the function without arguments will disable all per-sprite colors for the SpriteBatch.

In versions prior to 11.0, color component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

In version 0.9.2 and older, the global color set with love.graphics.setColor will not work on the SpriteBatch if any of the sprites has its own color.

arguments.name: g

constants.name: dynamic
constants.description: The object's data will change occasionally during its lifetime.

constants.name: static
constants.description: The object will not be modified after initial sprites or vertices are added.

constants.name: stream
constants.description: The object data will always change between draws.

constants.description: The transformation stack (love.graphics.translate, love.graphics.rotate, etc.)

constants.name: all
constants.description: All love.graphics state, including transform state.

entries.name: StencilAction
entries.description: How a stencil function modifies the stencil values of pixels it touches.

constants.name: replace
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be replaced by the value specified in love.graphics.stencil, if any object touches the pixel.

constants.name: increment
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be incremented by 1 for each object that touches the pixel. If the stencil value reaches 255 it will stay at 255.

constants.name: decrement
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be decremented by 1 for each object that touches the pixel. If the stencil value reaches 0 it will stay at 0.

constants.name: incrementwrap
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be incremented by 1 for each object that touches the pixel. If a stencil value of 255 is incremented it will be set to 0.

constants.name: decrementwrap
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be decremented by 1 for each object that touches the pixel. If the stencil value of 0 is decremented it will be set to 255.

constants.name: invert
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be bitwise-inverted for each object that touches the pixel. If a stencil value of 0 is inverted it will become 255.

entries.name: Texture
entries.description: Superclass for drawable objects which represent a texture. All Textures can be drawn with Quads. This is an abstract type that can't be created directly.

entries.name: getDepth
entries.description: Gets the depth of a Volume Texture. Returns 1 for 2D, Cubemap, and Array textures.

returns.name: depth
returns.description: The depth of the volume Texture.

entries.name: getDepthSampleMode
entries.description:
Gets the comparison mode used when sampling from a depth texture in a shader.

Depth texture comparison modes are advanced low-level functionality typically used with shadow mapping in 3D.

returns.name: compare
returns.description: The comparison mode used when sampling from this texture in a shader, or nil if setDepthSampleMode has not been called on this Texture.

entries.name: getDimensions
entries.description: Gets the width and height of the Texture.

returns.name: width
returns.description: The width of the Texture.

returns.name: height
returns.description: The height of the Texture.

entries.name: getDPIScale
entries.description:
Gets the DPI scale factor of the Texture.

The DPI scale factor represents relative pixel density. A DPI scale factor of 2 means the texture has twice the pixel density in each dimension (4 times as many pixels in the same area) compared to a texture with a DPI scale factor of 1.

For example, a texture with pixel dimensions of 100x100 with a DPI scale factor of 2 will be drawn as if it was 50x50. This is useful with high-dpi /  retina displays to easily allow swapping out higher or lower pixel density Images and Canvases without needing any extra manual scaling logic.

returns.name: dpiscale
returns.description: The DPI scale factor of the Texture.

entries.name: getFilter
entries.description: Gets the filter mode of the Texture.

returns.name: min
returns.description: Filter mode to use when minifying the texture (rendering it at a smaller size on-screen than its size in pixels).

returns.name: mag
returns.description: Filter mode to use when magnifying the texture (rendering it at a smaller size on-screen than its size in pixels).

returns.name: anisotropy
returns.description: Maximum amount of anisotropic filtering used.

entries.name: getFormat
entries.description: Gets the pixel format of the Texture.

returns.name: format
returns.description: The pixel format the Texture was created with.

entries.name: getHeight
entries.description: Gets the height of the Texture.

returns.name: height
returns.description: The height of the Texture.

entries.name: getLayerCount
entries.description: Gets the number of layers / slices in an Array Texture. Returns 1 for 2D, Cubemap, and Volume textures.

returns.name: layers
returns.description: The number of layers in the Array Texture.

entries.name: getMipmapCount
entries.description: Gets the number of mipmaps contained in the Texture. If the texture was not created with mipmaps, it will return 1.

returns.name: mipmaps
returns.description: The number of mipmaps in the Texture.

entries.name: getMipmapFilter
entries.description: Gets the mipmap filter mode for a Texture. Prior to 11.0 this method only worked on Images.

returns.name: mode
returns.description: The filter mode used in between mipmap levels. nil if mipmap filtering is not enabled.

returns.name: sharpness
returns.description: Value used to determine whether the image should use more or less detailed mipmap levels than normal when drawing.

entries.name: getPixelDimensions
entries.description:
Gets the width and height in pixels of the Texture.

Texture:getDimensions gets the dimensions of the texture in units scaled by the texture's DPI scale factor, rather than pixels. Use getDimensions for calculations related to drawing the texture (calculating an origin offset, for example), and getPixelDimensions only when dealing specifically with pixels, for example when using Canvas:newImageData.

returns.name: pixelwidth
returns.description: The width of the Texture, in pixels.

returns.name: pixelheight
returns.description: The height of the Texture, in pixels.

entries.name: getPixelHeight
entries.description:
Gets the height in pixels of the Texture.

DPI scale factor, rather than pixels. Use getHeight for calculations related to drawing the texture (calculating an origin offset, for example), and getPixelHeight only when dealing specifically with pixels, for example when using Canvas:newImageData.

returns.name: pixelheight
returns.description: The height of the Texture, in pixels.

entries.name: getPixelWidth
entries.description:
Gets the width in pixels of the Texture.

DPI scale factor, rather than pixels. Use getWidth for calculations related to drawing the texture (calculating an origin offset, for example), and getPixelWidth only when dealing specifically with pixels, for example when using Canvas:newImageData.

returns.name: pixelwidth
returns.description: The width of the Texture, in pixels.

entries.name: getTextureType
entries.description: Gets the type of the Texture.

returns.name: texturetype
returns.description: The type of the Texture.

entries.name: getWidth
entries.description: Gets the width of the Texture.

returns.name: width
returns.description: The width of the Texture.

entries.name: getWrap
entries.description:
Gets the wrapping properties of a Texture.

This function returns the currently set horizontal and vertical wrapping modes for the texture.

returns.name: horiz
returns.description: Horizontal wrapping mode of the texture.

returns.name: vert
returns.description: Vertical wrapping mode of the texture.

returns.name: depth
returns.description: Wrapping mode for the z-axis of a Volume texture.

entries.name: isReadable
entries.description:
Gets whether the Texture can be drawn and sent to a Shader.

Canvases created with stencil and/or depth PixelFormats are not readable by default, unless readable=true is specified in the settings table passed into love.graphics.newCanvas.

Non-readable Canvases can still be rendered to.

returns.name: readable
returns.description: Whether the Texture is readable.

entries.name: setDepthSampleMode
entries.description:
Sets the comparison mode used when sampling from a depth texture in a shader. Depth texture comparison modes are advanced low-level functionality typically used with shadow mapping in 3D.

When using a depth texture with a comparison mode set in a shader, it must be declared as a sampler2DShadow and used in a GLSL 3 Shader. The result of accessing the texture in the shader will return a float between 0 and 1, proportional to the number of samples (up to 4 samples will be used if bilinear filtering is enabled) that passed the test set by the comparison operation.

Depth texture comparison can only be used with readable depth-formatted Canvases.

arguments.name: compare
arguments.description: The comparison mode used when sampling from this texture in a shader.

entries.name: setFilter
entries.description: Sets the filter mode of the Texture.

arguments.name: min
arguments.description: Filter mode to use when minifying the texture (rendering it at a smaller size on-screen than its size in pixels).

arguments.name: mag
arguments.description: Filter mode to use when magnifying the texture (rendering it at a larger size on-screen than its size in pixels).

arguments.name: anisotropy
arguments.description: Maximum amount of anisotropic filtering to use.

entries.name: setMipmapFilter
entries.description:
Sets the mipmap filter mode for a Texture. Prior to 11.0 this method only worked on Images.

Mipmapping is useful when drawing a texture at a reduced scale. It can improve performance and reduce aliasing issues.

In created with the mipmaps flag enabled for the mipmap filter to have any effect. In versions prior to 0.10.0 it's best to call this method directly after creating the image with love.graphics.newImage, to avoid bugs in certain graphics drivers.

Due to hardware restrictions and driver bugs, in versions prior to 0.10.0 images that weren't loaded from a CompressedData must have power-of-two dimensions (64x64, 512x256, etc.) to use mipmaps.

variants.description: On mobile devices (Android and iOS), the sharpness parameter is not supported and will do nothing. You can use a custom compressed and its CompressedData has mipmap data included, it will use that.

arguments.name: filtermode
arguments.description: The filter mode to use in between mipmap levels. 'nearest' will often give better performance.

arguments.name: sharpness
arguments.description: A positive sharpness value makes the texture use a more detailed mipmap level when drawing, at the expense of performance. A negative value does the reverse.

variants.description: Disables mipmap filtering.

entries.name: setWrap
entries.description:
Sets the wrapping properties of a Texture.

This function sets the way a Texture is repeated when it is drawn with a Quad that is larger than the texture's extent, or when a custom Shader is used which uses texture coordinates outside of [0, 1]. A texture may be clamped or set to repeat in both horizontal and vertical directions.

Clamped textures appear only once (with the edges of the texture stretching to fill the extent of the Quad), whereas repeated ones repeat as many times as there is room in the Quad.

arguments.name: horiz
arguments.description: Horizontal wrapping mode of the texture.

arguments.name: vert
arguments.description: Vertical wrapping mode of the texture.

arguments.name: depth
arguments.description: Wrapping mode for the z-axis of a Volume texture.

entries.name: TextureType
entries.description: Types of textures (2D, cubemap, etc.)

constants.name: 2d
constants.description: Regular 2D texture with width and height.

constants.name: array
constants.description: Several same-size 2D textures organized into a single object. Similar to a texture atlas / sprite sheet, but avoids sprite bleeding and other issues.

constants.name: cube
constants.description: Cubemap texture with 6 faces. Requires a custom shader (and Shader:send) to use. Sampling from a cube texture in a shader takes a 3D direction vector instead of a texture coordinate.

constants.name: volume
constants.description: 3D texture with width, height, and depth. Requires a custom shader to use. Volume textures can have texture filtering applied along the 3rd axis.

entries.name: Thread
entries.description: A Thread is a chunk of code that can run in parallel with other threads. Data can be sent between different threads with Channel objects.

entries.name: getError
entries.description: Retrieves the error string from the thread if it produced an error.

returns.name: err
returns.description: The error message, or nil if the Thread has not caused an error.

entries.name: isRunning
entries.description:
Returns whether the thread is currently running.

Threads which are not running can be (re)started with Thread:start.

returns.name: value
returns.description: True if the thread is running, false otherwise.

entries.name: start
entries.description:
Starts the thread.

Beginning with version 0.9.0, threads can be restarted after they have completed their execution.

variants.description: Arguments passed to Thread:start are accessible in the thread's main file via '''...''' (the vararg expression.)

arguments.description: A string, number, boolean, LÖVE object, or simple table.

entries.name: wait
entries.description:
Wait for a thread to finish.

This call will block until the thread finishes.

entries.name: TimeUnit
entries.description: Units that represent time.

constants.name: seconds
constants.description: Regular seconds.

constants.name: samples
constants.description: Audio samples.

entries.name: Transform
entries.description:
Object containing a coordinate system transformation.

The love.graphics module has several functions and function variants which accept Transform objects.

entries.name: apply
entries.description:
Applies the given other Transform object to this one.

This effectively multiplies this Transform's internal transformation matrix with the other Transform's (i.e. self * other), and stores the result in this object.

arguments.name: other
arguments.description: The other Transform object to apply to this Transform.

returns.name: transform
returns.description: The Transform object the method was called on. Allows easily chaining Transform methods.

entries.name: clone
entries.description: Creates a new copy of this Transform.

returns.name: clone
returns.description: The copy of this Transform.

entries.name: getMatrix
entries.description: Gets the internal 4x4 transformation matrix stored by this Transform. The matrix is returned in row-major order.

returns.name: e1_1
returns.description: The first column of the first row of the matrix.

returns.name: e1_2
returns.description: The second column of the first row of the matrix.

returns.name: e1_3
returns.description: The third column of the first row of the matrix.

returns.name: e1_4
returns.description: The fourth column of the first row of the matrix.

returns.name: e2_1
returns.description: The first column of the second row of the matrix.

returns.name: e2_2
returns.description: The second column of the second row of the matrix.

returns.name: e2_3
returns.description: The third column of the second row of the matrix.

returns.name: e2_4
returns.description: The fourth column of the second row of the matrix.

returns.name: e3_1
returns.description: The first column of the third row of the matrix.

returns.name: e3_2
returns.description: The second column of the third row of the matrix.

returns.name: e3_3
returns.description: The third column of the third row of the matrix.

returns.name: e3_4
returns.description: The fourth column of the third row of the matrix.

returns.name: e4_1
returns.description: The first column of the fourth row of the matrix.

returns.name: e4_2
returns.description: The second column of the fourth row of the matrix.

returns.name: e4_3
returns.description: The third column of the fourth row of the matrix.

returns.name: e4_4
returns.description: The fourth column of the fourth row of the matrix.

entries.name: inverse
entries.description: Creates a new Transform containing the inverse of this Transform.

returns.name: inverse
returns.description: A new Transform object representing the inverse of this Transform's matrix.

entries.name: inverseTransformPoint
entries.description:
Applies the reverse of the Transform object's transformation to the given 2D position.

This effectively converts the given position from the local coordinate space of the Transform into global coordinates.

One use of this method can be to convert a screen-space mouse position into global world coordinates, if the given Transform has transformations applied that are used for a camera system in-game.

arguments.name: localX
arguments.description: The x component of the position with the transform applied.

arguments.name: localY
arguments.description: The y component of the position with the transform applied.

returns.name: globalX
returns.description: The x component of the position in global coordinates.

returns.name: globalY
returns.description: The y component of the position in global coordinates.

entries.name: isAffine2DTransform
entries.description: Checks whether the Transform is an affine transformation.

returns.name: affine
returns.description: true if the transform object is an affine transformation, false otherwise.

entries.name: reset
entries.description: Resets the Transform to an identity state. All previously applied transformations are erased.

returns.name: transform
returns.description: The Transform object the method was called on. Allows easily chaining Transform methods.

entries.name: rotate
entries.description: Applies a rotation to the Transform's coordinate system. This method does not reset any previously applied transformations.

arguments.name: angle
arguments.description: The relative angle in radians to rotate this Transform by.

returns.name: transform
returns.description: The Transform object the method was called on. Allows easily chaining Transform methods.

entries.name: scale
entries.description: Scales the Transform's coordinate system. This method does not reset any previously applied transformations.

arguments.name: sx
arguments.description: The relative scale factor along the x-axis.

arguments.name: sy
arguments.description: The relative scale factor along the y-axis.

returns.name: transform
returns.description: The Transform object the method was called on. Allows easily chaining Transform methods.

entries.name: setMatrix
entries.description: Directly sets the Transform's internal 4x4 transformation matrix.

arguments.name: e1_1

arguments.name: e1_2
arguments.description: The second column of the first row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e1_3

arguments.name: e1_4
arguments.description: The fourth column of the first row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e2_1
arguments.description: The first column of the second row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e2_2
arguments.description: The second column of the second row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e2_3
arguments.description: The third column of the second row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e2_4
arguments.description: The fourth column of the second row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e3_1
arguments.description: The first column of the third row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e3_2
arguments.description: The second column of the third row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e3_3
arguments.description: The third column of the third row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e3_4
arguments.description: The fourth column of the third row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e4_1
arguments.description: The first column of the fourth row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e4_2
arguments.description: The second column of the fourth row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e4_3
arguments.description: The third column of the fourth row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e4_4

returns.name: transform
returns.description: The Transform object the method was called on. Allows easily chaining Transform methods.

arguments.name: layout
arguments.description: How to interpret the matrix element arguments (row-major or column-major).

arguments.name: e1_1
arguments.description: The first column of the first row of the matrix.

arguments.name: e1_2
arguments.description: The second column of the first row or the first column of the second row of the matrix, depending on the specified layout.

arguments.name: e1_3
arguments.description: The third column/row of the first row/column of the matrix.

arguments.name: e1_4
arguments.description: The fourth column/row of the first row/column of the matrix.

arguments.name: e2_1
arguments.description: The first column/row of the second row/column of the matrix.

arguments.name: e2_2
arguments.description: The second column/row of the second row/column of the matrix.

arguments.name: e2_3
arguments.description: The third column/row of the second row/column of the matrix.

arguments.name: e2_4

arguments.name: e3_1

arguments.name: e3_2

arguments.name: e3_3
arguments.description: The third column/row of the third row/column of the matrix.

arguments.name: e3_4

arguments.name: e4_1

returns.name: transform

entries.name: setTransformation
entries.description: Resets the Transform to the specified transformation parameters.

arguments.name: y

arguments.name: angle

arguments.name: sx

arguments.name: sy

entries.name: shear
entries.description: Applies a shear factor (skew) to the Transform's coordinate system. This method does not reset any previously applied transformations.

arguments.name: kx
