arguments.name: ky
arguments.description: The shear factor along the y-axis.

returns.name: transform
returns.description: The Transform object the method was called on. Allows easily chaining Transform methods.

entries.name: transformPoint
entries.description:
Applies the Transform object's transformation to the given 2D position.

This effectively converts the given position from global coordinates into the local coordinate space of the Transform.

arguments.name: globalY
arguments.description: The y component of the position in global coordinates.

returns.name: localY

entries.description: Applies a translation to the Transform's coordinate system. This method does not reset any previously applied transformations.

arguments.name: dy

returns.name: transform
returns.description: The Transform object the method was called on. Allows easily chaining Transform methods.

entries.name: VertexAttributeStep
entries.description:
The frequency at which a vertex shader fetches the vertex attribute's data from the Mesh when it's drawn.

Per-instance attributes can be used to render a Mesh many times with different positions, colors, or other attributes via a single love.graphics.drawInstanced call, without using the love_InstanceID vertex shader variable.

constants.name: pervertex
constants.description: The vertex attribute will have a unique value for each vertex in the Mesh.

constants.name: perinstance
constants.description: The vertex attribute will have a unique value for each instance of the Mesh.

entries.name: VertexWinding
entries.description: How Mesh geometry vertices are ordered.

constants.name: cw
constants.description: Clockwise.

constants.name: ccw
constants.description: Counter-clockwise.

entries.name: Video
entries.description: A drawable video.

entries.name: getDimensions
entries.description: Gets the width and height of the Video in pixels.

returns.name: width
returns.description: The width of the Video.

returns.name: height
returns.description: The height of the Video.

entries.name: getFilter
entries.description: Gets the scaling filters used when drawing the Video.

returns.name: min
returns.description: The filter mode used when scaling the Video down.

returns.name: mag
returns.description: The filter mode used when scaling the Video up.

returns.name: anisotropy
returns.description: Maximum amount of anisotropic filtering used.

entries.name: getHeight
entries.description: Gets the height of the Video in pixels.

returns.name: height
returns.description: The height of the Video.

entries.name: getSource
entries.description: Gets the audio Source used for playing back the video's audio. May return nil if the video has no audio, or if Video:setSource is called with a nil argument.

returns.name: source
returns.description: The audio Source used for audio playback, or nil if the video has no audio.

entries.name: getStream
entries.description: Gets the VideoStream object used for decoding and controlling the video.

returns.name: stream
returns.description: The VideoStream used for decoding and controlling the video.

entries.name: getWidth
entries.description: Gets the width of the Video in pixels.

returns.name: width
returns.description: The width of the Video.

entries.name: isPlaying
entries.description: Gets whether the Video is currently playing.

returns.name: playing
returns.description: Whether the video is playing.

entries.name: pause
entries.description: Pauses the Video.

entries.name: play
entries.description: Starts playing the Video. In order for the video to appear onscreen it must be drawn with love.graphics.draw.

entries.name: rewind
entries.description: Rewinds the Video to the beginning.

entries.name: seek
entries.description: Sets the current playback position of the Video.

arguments.name: offset
arguments.description: The time in seconds since the beginning of the Video.

entries.name: setFilter
entries.description: Sets the scaling filters used when drawing the Video.

arguments.name: min
arguments.description: The filter mode used when scaling the Video down.

arguments.name: mag
arguments.description: The filter mode used when scaling the Video up.

arguments.name: anisotropy
arguments.description: Maximum amount of anisotropic filtering used.

entries.name: setSource
entries.description: Sets the audio Source used for playing back the video's audio. The audio Source also controls playback speed and synchronization.

arguments.name: source
arguments.description: The audio Source used for audio playback, or nil to disable audio synchronization.

entries.name: tell
entries.description: Gets the current playback position of the Video.

returns.name: seconds
returns.description: The time in seconds since the beginning of the Video.

entries.name: VideoStream
entries.description: An object which decodes, streams, and controls Videos.

entries.name: getFilename
entries.description: Gets the filename of the VideoStream.

returns.name: filename
returns.description: The filename of the VideoStream

entries.name: isPlaying
entries.description: Gets whether the VideoStream is playing.

returns.name: playing
returns.description: Whether the VideoStream is playing.

entries.name: pause
entries.description: Pauses the VideoStream.

entries.name: play
entries.description: Plays the VideoStream.

entries.name: rewind
entries.description: Rewinds the VideoStream. Synonym to VideoStream:seek(0).

entries.name: seek
entries.description: Sets the current playback position of the VideoStream.

arguments.name: offset
arguments.description: The time in seconds since the beginning of the VideoStream.

entries.name: tell
entries.description: Gets the current playback position of the VideoStream.

returns.name: seconds
returns.description: The number of seconds sionce the beginning of the VideoStream.

entries.name: WeldJoint
entries.description: A WeldJoint essentially glues two bodies together.

entries.name: getDampingRatio
entries.description: Returns the damping ratio of the joint.

returns.name: ratio
returns.description: The damping ratio.

entries.name: getFrequency
entries.description: Returns the frequency.

returns.name: freq
returns.description: The frequency in hertz.

entries.name: getReferenceAngle
entries.description: Gets the reference angle.

returns.name: angle
returns.description: The reference angle in radians.

entries.name: setDampingRatio
entries.description: Sets a new damping ratio.

arguments.name: ratio
arguments.description: The new damping ratio.

entries.name: setFrequency
entries.description: Sets a new frequency.

arguments.name: freq
arguments.description: The new frequency in hertz.

entries.name: WheelJoint
entries.description: Restricts a point on the second body to a line on the first body.

entries.name: getAxis
entries.description: Gets the world-space axis vector of the Wheel Joint.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The x-axis coordinate of the world-space axis vector.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The y-axis coordinate of the world-space axis vector.

entries.name: getJointSpeed
entries.description: Returns the current joint translation speed.

returns.name: speed
returns.description: The translation speed of the joint in meters per second.

entries.name: getJointTranslation
entries.description: Returns the current joint translation.

returns.name: position
returns.description: The translation of the joint in meters.

entries.name: getMaxMotorTorque
entries.description: Returns the maximum motor torque.

returns.name: maxTorque
returns.description: The maximum torque of the joint motor in newton meters.

entries.name: getMotorSpeed
entries.description: Returns the speed of the motor.

returns.name: speed
returns.description: The speed of the joint motor in radians per second.

entries.name: getMotorTorque
entries.description: Returns the current torque on the motor.

arguments.name: invdt
arguments.description: How long the force applies. Usually the inverse time step or 1/dt.

returns.name: torque
returns.description: The torque on the motor in newton meters.

entries.name: getSpringDampingRatio
entries.description: Returns the damping ratio.

returns.name: ratio
returns.description: The damping ratio.

entries.name: getSpringFrequency
entries.description: Returns the spring frequency.

returns.name: freq
returns.description: The frequency in hertz.

entries.name: isMotorEnabled
entries.description: Checks if the joint motor is running.

returns.name: on
returns.description: The status of the joint motor.

entries.name: setMaxMotorTorque
entries.description: Sets a new maximum motor torque.

arguments.name: maxTorque
arguments.description: The new maximum torque for the joint motor in newton meters.

entries.name: setMotorEnabled
entries.description: Starts and stops the joint motor.

arguments.name: enable
arguments.description: True turns the motor on and false turns it off.

entries.name: setMotorSpeed
entries.description: Sets a new speed for the motor.

arguments.name: speed
arguments.description: The new speed for the joint motor in radians per second.

entries.name: setSpringDampingRatio
entries.description: Sets a new damping ratio.

arguments.name: ratio
arguments.description: The new damping ratio.

entries.name: setSpringFrequency
entries.description: Sets a new spring frequency.

arguments.name: freq
arguments.description: The new frequency in hertz.

entries.name: World
entries.description: A world is an object that contains all bodies and joints.

entries.name: destroy
entries.description:
Destroys the world, taking all bodies, joints, fixtures and their shapes with it. 

An error will occur if you attempt to use any of the destroyed objects after calling this function.

entries.name: getBodies
entries.description: Returns a table with all bodies.

returns.name: bodies
returns.description: A sequence with all bodies.

entries.name: getBodyCount
entries.description: Returns the number of bodies in the world.

returns.name: n
returns.description: The number of bodies in the world.

entries.name: getCallbacks
entries.description: Returns functions for the callbacks during the world update.

returns.name: beginContact
returns.description: Gets called when two fixtures begin to overlap.

returns.name: endContact
returns.description: Gets called when two fixtures cease to overlap.

returns.name: preSolve
returns.description: Gets called before a collision gets resolved.

returns.name: postSolve
returns.description: Gets called after the collision has been resolved.

entries.name: getContactCount
entries.description: Returns the number of contacts in the world.

returns.name: n
returns.description: The number of contacts in the world.

entries.name: getContactFilter
entries.description: Returns the function for collision filtering.

returns.name: contactFilter
returns.description: The function that handles the contact filtering.

entries.name: getContacts
entries.description: Returns a table with all Contacts.

returns.name: contacts
returns.description: A sequence with all Contacts.

entries.name: getGravity
entries.description: Get the gravity of the world.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The x component of gravity.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The y component of gravity.

entries.name: getJointCount
entries.description: Returns the number of joints in the world.

returns.name: n
returns.description: The number of joints in the world.

entries.name: getJoints
entries.description: Returns a table with all joints.

returns.name: joints
returns.description: A sequence with all joints.

entries.name: isDestroyed
entries.description: Gets whether the World is destroyed. Destroyed worlds cannot be used.

returns.name: destroyed
returns.description: Whether the World is destroyed.

entries.name: isLocked
entries.description:
Returns if the world is updating its state.

This will return true inside the callbacks from World:setCallbacks.

returns.name: locked
returns.description: Will be true if the world is in the process of updating its state.

entries.name: isSleepingAllowed
entries.description: Gets the sleep behaviour of the world.

returns.name: allow
returns.description: True if bodies in the world are allowed to sleep, or false if not.

entries.name: queryBoundingBox
entries.description: Calls a function for each fixture inside the specified area by searching for any overlapping bounding box (Fixture:getBoundingBox).

arguments.name: topLeftX
arguments.description: The x position of the top-left point.

arguments.name: topLeftY
arguments.description: The y position of the top-left point.

arguments.name: bottomRightX
arguments.description: The x position of the bottom-right point.

arguments.name: bottomRightY
arguments.description: The y position of the bottom-right point.

arguments.name: callback
arguments.description: This function gets passed one argument, the fixture, and should return a boolean. The search will continue if it is true or stop if it is false.

entries.name: rayCast
entries.description: Casts a ray and calls a function for each fixtures it intersects.

variants.description: There is a bug in LÖVE 0.8.0 where the normal vector passed to the callback function gets scaled by love.physics.getMeter.

arguments.name: x1
arguments.description: The x position of the starting point of the ray.

arguments.name: y1
arguments.description: The x position of the starting point of the ray.

arguments.name: x2
arguments.description: The x position of the end point of the ray.

arguments.name: y2
arguments.description: The x value of the surface normal vector of the shape edge.

arguments.name: callback
arguments.description: A function called for each fixture intersected by the ray. The function gets six arguments and should return a number as a control value. The intersection points fed into the function will be in an arbitrary order. If you wish to find the closest point of intersection, you'll need to do that yourself within the function. The easiest way to do that is by using the fraction value.

entries.name: setCallbacks
entries.description:
Sets functions for the collision callbacks during the world update.

Four Lua functions can be given as arguments. The value nil removes a function.

When called, each function will be passed three arguments. The first two arguments are the colliding fixtures and the third argument is the Contact between them. The postSolve callback additionally gets the normal and tangent impulse for each contact point. See notes.

If you are interested to know when exactly each callback is called, consult a Box2d manual

arguments.name: beginContact
arguments.description: Gets called when two fixtures begin to overlap.

arguments.name: endContact
arguments.description: Gets called when two fixtures cease to overlap. This will also be called outside of a world update, when colliding objects are destroyed.

arguments.name: preSolve
arguments.description: Gets called before a collision gets resolved.

arguments.name: postSolve
arguments.description: Gets called after the collision has been resolved.

entries.name: setContactFilter
entries.description:
Sets a function for collision filtering.

If the group and category filtering doesn't generate a collision decision, this function gets called with the two fixtures as arguments. The function should return a boolean value where true means the fixtures will collide and false means they will pass through each other.

arguments.name: filter
arguments.description: The function handling the contact filtering.

entries.name: setGravity
entries.description: Set the gravity of the world.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x component of gravity.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y component of gravity.

entries.name: setSleepingAllowed
entries.description: Sets the sleep behaviour of the world.

arguments.name: allow
arguments.description: True if bodies in the world are allowed to sleep, or false if not.

entries.name: translateOrigin
entries.description: Translates the World's origin. Useful in large worlds where floating point precision issues become noticeable at far distances from the origin.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x component of the new origin with respect to the old origin.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y component of the new origin with respect to the old origin.

entries.name: update
entries.description: Update the state of the world.

arguments.name: dt
arguments.description: The time (in seconds) to advance the physics simulation.

arguments.name: velocityiterations
arguments.description: The maximum number of steps used to determine the new velocities when resolving a collision.

arguments.name: positioniterations
arguments.description: The maximum number of steps used to determine the new positions when resolving a collision.

entries.name: WrapMode
entries.description: How the image wraps inside a Quad with a larger quad size than image size. This also affects how Meshes with texture coordinates which are outside the range of 1 are drawn, and the color returned by the Texel Shader function when using it to sample from texture coordinates outside of the range of 1.

constants.name: clamp
constants.description: Clamp the texture. Appears only once. The area outside the texture's normal range is colored based on the edge pixels of the texture.

constants.name: repeat
constants.description: Repeat the texture. Fills the whole available extent.

constants.name: mirroredrepeat
constants.description: Repeat the texture, flipping it each time it repeats. May produce better visual results than the repeat mode when the texture doesn't seamlessly tile.

constants.name: clampzero
constants.description: Clamp the texture. Fills the area outside the texture's normal range with transparent black (or opaque black for textures with no alpha channel.)
